# ID-28: Cocoindex flow scaffolding + Cloud Run sidecar deploy (T8)

Stand up the cocoindex 1.0.3 pipeline scaffolding for the canonical document → embeddings flow, and the Cloud Run sidecar that hosts the heavy extractor binaries (Docling 1.8 GB + pullmd 3.7 GB Playwright). T8 is the foundational pipeline implementation step in `docs/specs/canonical-pipeline-implementation-plan/PLAN.md` §4.8 — downstream T7 (Phew Q&A first-ingest) depends on T8 standing up the engine boundary. Multi-session arc: S252 authors the full {N.1}–{N.4} spec chain (RESEARCH → PRODUCT → TECH → PLAN) only. Implementation Subtasks {28.5+} dispatch in S253+ after PLAN.md ratifies.

## Acceptance criteria

- Subtask {28.1} RESEARCH.md captures cocoindex 1.0.3 integration constraints (sandbox-disable + walk_dir recursive=True default + layered fn-shape COCO.10), Cloud Run sidecar deploy shape (Docling + pullmd + Playwright sizing), op_id propagation per `02-data-flow.md` §5 / N7, Spike #1 (cocoindex schema-coupling Scenario A) + Spike #2 status, and single-spec vs multi-spec decision.
- Subtask {28.2} PRODUCT.md numbered Behavior invariants covering: cocoindex flow observability (localfs → 6-stage pipeline → `content_items` UPSERT); sidecar deploy contract (Docling + pullmd availability at engine boundary); op_id propagation contract for audit trail; `recordPipelineRun` rollup per `02-data-flow.md` §7.2; ExtractByLlm contract per Q-EX2 (T1 dep); failure-mode behaviour (retry / dead-letter / observability).
- Subtask {28.3} TECH.md one-to-one Proposed changes per PRODUCT invariant: `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` scaffolding; outer-tier `@coco.fn` source-binding adapter; inner-tier extractors consuming `content_text: str` (COCO.10); `ExtractByLlm` + `LiteLLMEmbedder` + `postgres.mount_table_target(managed_by='user')`; Cloud Run sidecar Dockerfile + `cloud-run-deploy.yml`; op_id columns + audit_log trigger; integration test design.
- Subtask {28.4} PLAN.md decomposes TECH into TM-shape implementation Subtasks {28.5+} with load-bearing details + one-line testStrategy + sibling-only deps.

## Dependencies

T1 spec ratification (Q-EX2 ExtractByLlm contract) — RESEARCH may surface T12 dependency shape; flag if so. No hard cross-Task blockers in main-tree task-list at session-start (production-readiness has ID-24/25/26 pending which may also surface during merge cadence).

### ID-28.5: Migration — op_id column propagation + B-tree partial indexes on 4 tables

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

DDL migration adding op_id uuid NULL + B-tree partial index on content_items, q_a_extractions, source_documents, pipeline_runs. IF NOT EXISTS-guarded; BLOCKER for op_id-consuming Subtasks 28.8/28.9/28.11.

**Test strategy:** Post-apply information_schema query returns 4 op_id columns; to_regclass('public.pipeline_failures') IS NULL guard holds; type regen committed.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.5 (Migration: op_id column propagation)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-4 (migration half).
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-11 (per-row op_id stamping substrate), Inv-12 (op_id round-trip column substrate).
**Sibling deps:** none — first Wave A node, no upstream blockers.

### Goal
Land the schema substrate for cocoindex per-flow op_id stamping. ADD COLUMN-only; no PL/pgSQL functions; no destructive operations. Single migration file under `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_t8_op_id_propagation.sql`.

### Files to create
- `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_t8_op_id_propagation.sql` — DDL per TECH §P-4 sketch:
  - `SET search_path = public, extensions;` (top of file)
  - `ALTER TABLE public.content_items ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS op_id uuid NULL;`
  - `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_content_items_op_id ON public.content_items (op_id) WHERE op_id IS NOT NULL;`
  - Same pattern for `q_a_extractions`, `source_documents`, `pipeline_runs` (4 tables total).
  - `COMMENT ON COLUMN` for all 4 columns naming the spec reference.

### Apply procedure (per TECH §3)
1. Pre-push: `cat supabase/.temp/project-ref` — must match expected project (`turayklvaunphgbgscat` for staging, `rovrymhhffssilaftdwd` for prod). Relink via `supabase link --project-ref <correct>` if drift detected (CLAUDE.md gotcha).
2. Pre-push column-existence verification via `information_schema.columns` query — IF NOT EXISTS no-ops are safe but worth confirming first run is the first time.
3. Apply via `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase db push` with `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` (POSTGRES_PASSWORD env var set).
4. Post-apply: re-run the column query; assert all 4 columns present.
5. Type regen: `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase gen types typescript --project-id rovrymhhffssilaftdwd --schema public > supabase/types/database.types.ts` and commit.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- DDL via CLI only (NOT MCP `apply_migration` / `execute_sql`).
- IF NOT EXISTS guards on every ALTER for idempotency.
- No PL/pgSQL functions in this migration so the anon-EXECUTE-via-PUBLIC pattern is NOT triggered — but if v1.1 follow-up adds e.g. `audit_log_emit(...)` helper, that migration MUST apply: REVOKE ... FROM PUBLIC + REVOKE ... FROM anon + GRANT ... TO authenticated, service_role.
- `.temp/project-ref` drift verification per CLAUDE.md gotcha.
- Index creation uses default (non-CONCURRENTLY) — v1 corpus scale completes in <1 s. If prod scale crosses 1M rows on any of the 4 tables, swap to CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY and split into two migrations.

### Acceptance test shape
- Post-apply: `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND column_name='op_id' AND table_name IN ('content_items','q_a_extractions','source_documents','pipeline_runs');` returns 4 rows.
- Post-apply: `\d pipeline_failures` returns 'relation does not exist' (Inv-19 invariant preserved — no new KH-side dead-letter table introduced).
- `docs/reference/SCHEMA-QUICK-REFERENCE.md` bumped with op_id column entries on the 4 tables (hygiene per CLAUDE.md self-policed reference-doc freshness).

### ID-28.6: Cloud Run sidecar Service deploy infra (4 per-tenant Services + Docling pre-warm)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

4 per-tenant Cloud Run Service manifests, new cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml, Docling pre-warm script, requirements.txt pins, .github/workflows/cloud-run-deploy.yml extension. ~5.3 GB image budget (log+warn per T-OQ4). pullmd remains separate per O-Q3 AGPL boundary.

**Test strategy:** All 4 Service manifests deploy via `gcloud run services replace`; health probe returns 200; docker inspect confirms zero pullmd binaries; IMAGE_SHA env var surfaces in `pipeline_runs.result.extractor_version` after first run.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.6 (Cloud Run sidecar Service deploy)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-1.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-6, Inv-7, Inv-8, Inv-9, Inv-10.
**Sibling deps:** none — Wave A parallel node alongside 28.5 and 28.7.

### Goal
Add 4 per-tenant Cloud Run **Services** (NOT Jobs per O-Q2 ratification) alongside the existing per-tenant Jobs at `cloudrun/jobs/{prod,staging}-{kpf,phew}.yaml`. Services host cocoindex engine (Rust + LMDB) + Docling (1.8 GB MIT) in one container image. pullmd remains a separately-deployed Service per O-Q3 AGPL boundary — the cocoindex sidecar calls pullmd via HTTP.

### Files to create
- `cloudrun/services/{prod,staging}-{kpf,phew}-cocoindex.yaml` (4 files). Each:
  - `min_instances: 1, max_instances: 1` per S14 single-orchestrator-instance topology.
  - `cpu: 2, memory: 4Gi` (Docling model in memory; ~5.3 GB image needs headroom).
  - `containerConcurrency: 10` (cocoindex single-writer LMDB; in-process concurrency only).
  - `serviceAccountName: {phew,kpf}-pipeline-sa@...` (REUSE existing per-tenant SAs).
  - Secret Manager mounts via `--set-secrets` (reuse existing 11-secret loop in workflow).
  - Env vars: `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` (empty default per O-Q8), `IMAGE_SHA` (injected by Cloud Build), `PIPELINE_RUN_WEBHOOK_URL` (mounted via Secret Manager — consumed by 28.11), `PULLMD_SERVICE_URL` (mounted; consumed by 28.7's _pullmd_to_markdown call).
- `cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml` — second cloudbuild config building a new image `kh-cocoindex-pipeline` (separate from existing `kh-pipeline`). Buildpack `gcr.io/buildpacks/python` + extra Docling pre-warm step that downloads layout-heron + docling-models at build time (NOT runtime) per O-Q4 cold-start tolerance.
  - Image-size budget: ~5.3 GB target.
  - **T-OQ4 ratified: log+warn at v1** (NOT fail-build). Emit warning if image >5.3 GB; ratchet to hard-enforce in T13 if bloat becomes load-bearing.
  - Post-build assertion step: `docker image inspect` returns zero `pullmd` binary entries + zero Playwright dependencies (Inv-9 enforcement).
- `cloudrun/cocoindex-prewarm.py` — build-time script: imports Docling and triggers model download (`docling.document_converter.DocumentConverter()` lazy-loads on first call; pre-warm forces fetch into image layer). Runs under `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` per CLAUDE.md cocoindex gotcha.
- `cloudrun/services/README.md` — operator-facing doc: Service vs Job split rationale (S14 + O-Q2), per-tenant SA mapping, smoke-verify command (`gcloud run services describe kh-cocoindex-pipeline-{phew,kpf}`), pre-warm-vs-runtime-download trade-off.

### Files to edit
- `.github/workflows/cloud-run-deploy.yml`:
  - Add job step `Build cocoindex image via Cloud Build` (parallel to existing `Build images via Cloud Build`, using `--config=cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml`).
  - Add step `Deploy cocoindex Services` (parallel to existing `Deploy tenant manifests`, using `gcloud run services replace cloudrun/services/${ENV_PREFIX}-${TENANT}-cocoindex.yaml`).
  - Reuse same WIF auth + deploy SA + Secret Manager mount step — extend secrets loop to cover both Job and Service deploys.
  - Path-trigger guard: add `cloudrun/services/**` to `paths:` allow-list.
- `requirements.txt`:
  - `cocoindex[postgres]==1.0.3` (validated by S1/S2/S14 spikes).
  - `docling>=2.0.0,<3.0.0` (cap minor-version range; pinned exact at first deploy per O-Q4 cold-start tolerance).
  - `litellm>=1.40.0,<2.0.0` (per O-Q5 ratified default; verify Anthropic + OpenAI passthrough works under WIF SA at first deploy).
  - `asyncpg>=0.30.0` (cocoindex transitive; explicit-pin for reproducibility).

### Sidecar version identification (Inv-8 specific wiring)
Cloud Build pipeline injects `IMAGE_SHA` (resolved git commit SHA at build time) as a Cloud Run Service env var; cocoindex sidecar reads `os.environ['IMAGE_SHA']` at boot and passes it through to every `recordPipelineRun()` call (via the webhook from 28.11) in the `result.extractor_version` field. Forensic correlation per Inv-8: query `pipeline_runs.result -> 'extractor_version'` for any successful run; cross-reference the SHA against the Cloud Run deploy log via `gcloud run services describe kh-cocoindex-pipeline-{tenant} --format='value(spec.template.spec.containers[0].env)'`.

### T-OQ3 ratified — 4 per-tenant Services
Per-tenant isolation at the Cloud Run boundary is a load-bearing security property (data-residency + multi-tenant compromise blast-radius). Collapsing to 2 Services (one per env) breaks that. Per-tenant Cloud Run Service cost is small; isolation is worth more.

### LMDB cold-start strategy (O-Q6 ratified)
LMDB ops-DB is ephemeral per container instance — no Cloud Storage persistent volume mount at v1. On cold-start, cocoindex re-fingerprints the source corpus (~7 s for 35-file v1 canonical scale per Spike #2 measurement). Re-evaluate if corpus crosses 10 k files per v1.1 guidance.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- Bun fetch hangs on HTTP 204 through sandbox proxy — does not affect deploy infra but is relevant for 28.14 integration tests.
- cocoindex requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for pre-warm script execution.

### Acceptance test shape
- **Inv-6:** HTTPS health probe to `https://<service-url>/health` returns 200 OK from Vercel host within Cloud Run warm-start window.

### ID-28.7: Layered fn-shape per-MIME adapters (scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/adapters.py)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Outer-tier @coco.fn(memo=True) convert_binary_to_markdown(file) + three inner-tier extractors: _docling_to_markdown(bytes), _pullmd_to_markdown(url), _passthrough_markdown(str). Inner-tier signatures take bytes/str — never FileLike — so memoisation key is content-hash.

**Test strategy:** convert_binary_to_markdown signature matches FileLike->str; inner-tier functions take bytes/str (verified via type-annotation grep — never FileLike); memo-hit integration test (28.14) confirms second-ingest is no-op.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.7 (Layered fn-shape per-MIME adapters)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-3.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-4 (idempotency via content-hash memoisation).
**Sibling deps:** none — Wave A parallel node alongside 28.5 and 28.6.

### Goal
Author the **outer-tier** `@coco.fn(memo=True)` source-binding adapter `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` AND the **inner-tier** per-MIME extractor `@coco.fn(memo=True)` functions consuming `content_text: str` per S9 §7.2 layered fn-shape (COCO.10).

### Why the layering matters
Inner-tier extractors take `bytes` (Docling) or `str` (pullmd URL, passthrough) so memoisation key is **content bytes, not file handles** — metadata-only edits (mtime, owner) do not re-trigger inner work. Outer-tier `FileLike` arg lets cocoindex select the inner-tier by MIME dispatch; memoisation at both tiers means a binary with unchanged content but rewrapped on disk is a memo-hit on the inner tier even if the outer tier's file-handle changed.

### Files to create
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/adapters.py` — module with:
  - `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` — outer-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. Routes by MIME:
    - PDF/DOCX/XLSX → calls `_docling_to_markdown(content_bytes: bytes) -> str`
    - HTML → calls `_pullmd_to_markdown(url: str) -> str`
    - markdown → calls `_passthrough_markdown(content_text: str) -> str`
  - `_docling_to_markdown(content_bytes: bytes) -> str` — inner-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. Imports `docling.document_converter.DocumentConverter` (pre-warmed in image layer per 28.6 P-1).
  - `_pullmd_to_markdown(url: str) -> str` — inner-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. HTTP POST to pullmd Service URL (`PULLMD_SERVICE_URL` env var, mounted via Cloud Run Secret Manager per 28.6).
  - `_passthrough_markdown(content_text: str) -> str` — inner-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. Identity transform; memoisation buys idempotency on content-hash match.

### Pattern reference (mirror this shape)
`scripts/ontology-sync/parse-flow.py` — canonical outer/inner split. Outer tier takes the file argument; inner tier takes the content payload. Inner-tier signatures MUST take `bytes` (Docling) or `str` (pullmd URL, passthrough markdown) — **NEVER** `FileLike` per S9 §7.2 (would break content-hash memoisation).

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- cocoindex 1.0.3 requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for both PyPI install and Rust-engine LMDB startup in dev.
- `localfs.walk_dir` defaults `recursive=False` — but this is consumed by 28.8 (flow.py), not here. Adapters operate on the file argument cocoindex passes in.

### Acceptance test shape (handed off to 28.14)
- **Inv-4** (memo-hit determinism): integration test ingests a file twice without modification; asserts `q_a_extractions` row count for that source is identical pre/post second run AND the second run's `pipeline_runs.items_created` is empty (memo-hit short-circuits all inner-tier work). Mirrors S2 spike Run B (warm-cache = 0 invocations).
- **Inv-5** (nested-corpus recursive support): covered by 28.8's flow.py acceptance test (`localfs.walk_dir(recursive=True)`) — the recursive adapter chain works through any depth.

### Function signature lock
The outer-tier signature `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` is consumed by 28.8 (`flow.py` imports it). If 28.7 and 28.8 are dispatched in parallel Wave A, this signature MUST be locked before 28.8 starts Wave B — orchestrator gate.

<info added on 2026-05-21T12:00:00.000Z>

Created `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__init__.py` (package marker), `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/adapters.py` (outer-tier `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` dispatcher + three inner-tier converters), and `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_adapters.py` (26 tests covering all MIME paths, memoisation, shape invariants, and the FileLike API deviation).
Commit: 21be6efe (full SHA: 21be6efeb8d9cfa6afe9bc1a9ef48900855c4c3f — verify with git log).
Spec slice: TECH.md T8 §7.2 (COCO.10 layered fn-shape + memoisation key invariant).
In-flight discoveries:
  - cocoindex 1.0.3 API deviation from brief: `FileLike` exposes `.file_path.path` (PurePath) + async `read()`/`read_text()`, NOT `.mime_type`/`.url`/`.read_bytes()` as assumed. MIME is derived from `.suffix`; HTML url derived from `str(file.file_path.path)`. Layered shape + memoisation key invariant fully preserved per S9 §7.2 (COCO.10).
  - Inner-tier signature lock confirmed for 28.8 handoff: `_docling_to_markdown(content_bytes: bytes)`, `_pullmd_to_markdown(url: str)`, `_passthrough_markdown(content_text: str)` — NONE accept FileLike directly.
  - All 26 tests pass under `python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_adapters.py`.
  - Status flipped pending → in_progress at first commit.

- **Status:** done

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.8 (Cocoindex flow.py 6-stage scaffolding)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-2.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-1 (file-change detection), Inv-2 (end-to-end latency), Inv-3 (six-stage topology observable), Inv-5 (nested-corpus coverage).
**Sibling deps:** [5, 6, 7] — op_id columns must exist (28.5), Cloud Run Service env vars + secrets must be deployable (28.6), `convert_binary_to_markdown` adapter must exist for the flow.py import to resolve (28.7).

### Goal
Author the canonical 6-stage cocoindex flow per `02-data-flow.md` §3.1 + Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 flow-scope pattern. Module exports `app_main()` async function + `KH_PIPELINE_APP = coco.AppConfig(name='kh_pipeline', main_fn=app_main)`. Source-binding via `localfs.walk_dir(live=True, recursive=True)` per CLAUDE.md gotcha + RESEARCH §1.2. Six stages wired flow-scope (NOT `@coco.fn`-wrapped — per Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 verifier B-3 finding).

### Files to create
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__init__.py` — empty package marker.
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — module per TECH §P-2 sketch (full Python shape is in TECH.md §P-2 — implement verbatim with these key elements):
  - Imports: `cocoindex as coco`, `localfs`, `ColumnDef`, `TableSchema`, `mount_table_target`, `ManagedBy`, `LlmSpec`, `LlmApiType`, `ExtractByLlm`, `LiteLLMEmbedder`, and the adapter from 28.7 (`convert_binary_to_markdown`).
  - Cross-link imports for Q-EX2 (consumed in 28.12, not here): `ClassificationExtraction`, `EntityMentionExtraction`, `QAFormExtraction` from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py`; instruction prompts from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/prompts.py`. These modules are authored by T1.2 — ALREADY LANDED.
  - 3 TableSchema declarations: `CONTENT_ITEMS_SCHEMA`, `Q_A_EXTRACTIONS_SCHEMA`, `SOURCE_DOCUMENTS_SCHEMA`. **OMIT `content_text_hash` from CONTENT_ITEMS_SCHEMA** (GENERATED ALWAYS per CLAUDE.md gotcha). Include `op_id` ColumnDef on all 3.
  - `async def app_main():` body:
    - Read `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` env var. If unset/empty per O-Q8, log idle-mode message and return (T8 ships empty source-binding; T7 stages files post-T8 stable).
    - Create asyncpg pool via `_build_dsn()`.
    - Stage 1 source walk: `flow['source'] = localfs.walk_dir(source_path, live=True, recursive=True)`.
    - Stage 2 binary conversion: `flow['content_text'] = flow['source'].transform(convert_binary_to_markdown)`.
    - Stages 3 (LLM extraction) — placeholder transforms for `classification`, `q_a_form`, `entity_mentions`. **Real ExtractByLlm wiring lands in 28.12** — at 28.8 the calls can be stubbed or commented-out for the test path to pass module-load.
    - Stage 4 embedding: `flow['embedding'] = flow['content_text'].transform(LiteLLMEmbedder(model='openai/text-embedding-3-large'))`.
    - Stage 5 entity resolution: `flow['resolved_entities'] = flow['entity_mentions'].transform(coco.functions.entity_resolution())`.
    - Stage 6 Postgres UPSERT: 3 `mount_table_target(..., managed_by=ManagedBy.USER)` calls + 3 `flow.bind_target(...)` calls. **op_id stamping ADDED IN 28.9** — at 28.8 the bind_target calls land without the op_id kwarg.
  - `_build_dsn() -> str` helper — mirrors `spike/cocoindex_s1/probe_managed_by_user.py:build_dsn()`. Reads `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` from env (mounted via Cloud Run Secret Manager per 28.6).
  - Module-level constant `KH_PIPELINE_APP = coco.AppConfig(name='kh_pipeline', main_fn=app_main)`.
  - `if __name__ == '__main__':` guard: `asyncio.run(coco.start(KH_PIPELINE_APP))`.
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__main__.py` — module entrypoint so `python3 -m scripts.cocoindex_pipeline` boots the App (used by Cloud Run Service `GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT` per 28.6 manifest).

### Latency budgets baked in (PRODUCT Inv-2 + P-OQ4 dual budget)
- 35-file canonical corpus end-to-end ≤ 120 s (S2 cold-cache baseline).
- Per-file p95 ≤ 30 s (protects against pathological inputs).
Both metrics observable via `pipeline_runs.completed_at - pipeline_runs.started_at` after 28.11 webhook lands.

### O-Q8 empty-source idle-mode behaviour
On Service boot, if `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` is unset OR points at a missing folder, log info and exit `app_main()` cleanly. The Service stays running (Cloud Run expects long-lived process); cocoindex's live-fs-watch arms once source-binding is set. T7 Phew first-ingest cutover sets `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` and restarts the Service post-T8-stable. **Do NOT raise** — that would crash the Service and trigger Cloud Run restart loop.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- `localfs.walk_dir(recursive=True)` — default is False; explicit True for nested corpora per CLAUDE.md cocoindex gotcha + RESEARCH §1.2.
- `content_items.content_text_hash` is GENERATED ALWAYS — omit from TableSchema.
- cocoindex 1.0.3 requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for both PyPI install and Rust-engine LMDB startup in dev.
- Embedding vector serialisation: `JSON.stringify(embedding)` for Supabase RPC vector params; cocoindex's `LiteLLMEmbedder` handles this internally.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-1:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/file-change-detection.integration.test.ts`
- **Inv-2:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/latency-budget.integration.test.ts`
- **Inv-3:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/stage-topology.integration.test.ts`
- **Inv-5:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/nested-corpus.integration.test.ts`

### ID-28.9: op_id flow-scope stamping (Python) + record-run.ts opId extension

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.5, ID-28.8
- **Updated:** _unset_

Augment 28.8's flow.bind_target() calls to pass op_id=flow['op_id'] so cocoindex stamps each UPSERTed row. Edit lib/pipeline/record-run.ts to add opId field to RecordPipelineRunParams + sb() insert payload.

**Test strategy:** After single ingest run, content_items/q_a_extractions/source_documents all show matching op_id; pipeline_runs.op_id matches; Inv-12 round-trip query returns exactly one row; memo-hit re-ingest produces no new content_items.updated_at change.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.9 (op_id flow-scope stamping)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-4 (code half — 28.5 covered the migration half).
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-11 (per-row op_id stamping), Inv-12 (op_id round-trip), Inv-14 (non-pipeline writes still audit-logged with NULL op_id), Inv-15 (no-op writes don't produce audit-log noise via memo-hit short-circuit).
**Sibling deps:** [5, 8] — op_id columns must exist (28.5); flow.py bind_target calls must be authored (28.8).

### Goal
Wire cocoindex's per-flow op_id into the Postgres UPSERT path so every row written by a pipeline run carries the originating run's op_id. Extend the TS-side `recordPipelineRun()` helper to accept and persist op_id on `pipeline_runs.op_id` column.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — augment the 3 `flow.bind_target()` calls from 28.8 so cocoindex stamps `op_id` on every UPSERT:
  ```python
  flow.bind_target(ci_target, key_fields=('id',),
                   value=flow['embedding'], op_id=flow['op_id'])
  flow.bind_target(qa_target, key_fields=('id',),
                   value=flow['q_a_form'], op_id=flow['op_id'])
  flow.bind_target(sd_target, key_fields=('id',),
                   value=flow['source'], op_id=flow['op_id'])
  ```
  The `flow['op_id']` reference is cocoindex's per-flow op_id symbol — assigned at flow construction time by the engine, identical across all 3 bind_target calls in the same flow invocation (Inv-11 guarantee: all 3 op_ids match each other).
- `lib/pipeline/record-run.ts` — extend `RecordPipelineRunParams` interface:
  ```typescript
  export interface RecordPipelineRunParams {
    // ... existing fields ...
    opId?: string | null;  // cocoindex per-flow op_id; uuid string
  }
  ```
  Update the `sb()` insert payload to include `op_id: opId ?? null`. Use `sb()` (fail-fast) from `@/lib/supabase/safe` — never raw supabase.from(...).insert (Inv-18 discipline).

### Inv-14 substrate (non-pipeline writes)
Inv-14 is a Postgres-side invariant (any write to a governed table produces an `audit_log` row regardless of writer). At v1 per P-OQ1 ratification: `audit_log` table population DEFERRED-v1.1; v1 substrate is structured logs (28.10). Inv-14 verification at v1 is via log-line inspection — direct UI edit produces a log line with NULL op_id, populated table-name + row-id + operation-type + invoking-role.

### Inv-15 substrate (no-op writes)
Inv-15 follows from Inv-4's memo-hit short-circuit (28.7 layered fn-shape). When content-hash matches, cocoindex does NOT issue a Postgres UPDATE — so no `updated_at` change, no audit-log line, no op_id stamping noise. Verifiable: re-ingest unchanged file twice; assert `pipeline_runs` count = 2 (per Inv-16) but `content_items.updated_at` and audit-log line count stay constant.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- REST PATCH on wrong UUID returns 200 OK with 0 rows (silent no-op). Always verify updates by re-querying (relevant for 28.14 acceptance test).
- `classifyContent` userId must be a UUID — pipeline service account `a0000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001`, never literal strings. cocoindex's per-flow op_id is a fresh uuid v4 generated by the engine — no userId coupling.
- Silent failures in Supabase calls: use `sb()` from `@/lib/supabase/safe` (already the recordPipelineRun helper's pattern); enforced by ESLint rule `local/no-unchecked-supabase-error`.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-11 + Inv-12:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/op-id-stamping.integration.test.ts` — ingest one file; query all 3 tables for the produced rows; assert all op_id columns equal each other AND equal pipeline_runs.op_id for the run; round-trip: SELECT pipeline_runs WHERE op_id = <value> returns exactly one row.
- **Inv-14:** `__tests__/integration/audit/non-pipeline-write.integration.test.ts` — direct UI edit via service-role client; assert log line with NULL op_id appears (v1 substrate; v1.1 will replace with audit_log SELECT).
- **Inv-15:** part of `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/memo-hit-pipeline-run.integration.test.ts` — re-ingest unchanged file; assert no new content_items.updated_at change AND no new audit-log line.

Per P-OQ1 ratification (S252 W2 close), v1 does NOT populate an `audit_log` table for pipeline-driven writes. The v1 audit-observability path is **structured logs** (Cloud Run sidecar log ingest per RLS-PATTERN P-5 `[DEFERRED-v1.1]`). Inv-13 is retained as the behaviour-contract that v1.1 MUST satisfy when the `audit_log` table substrate lands.

  ```python
  coco.logger.info(json.dumps({
      'event': 'cocoindex.upsert',
      'op_id': str(op_id),
      'table': table_name,
      'row_id': str(row_id),
      'operation': 'INSERT' | 'UPDATE',  # cocoindex exposes via callback

  ```
  Cloud Run's log ingest surface auto-parses JSON-formatted log lines into `jsonPayload` — no new KH-side log shipping plumbing needed.

**Deviations from brief — API deviation S255 (cocoindex 1.0.3 has no per-row UPSERT callback at bind_target):**
The brief directs emission `at the Stage 6 bind_target completion hook (cocoindex per-row UPSERT callback)`. Empirically, cocoindex 1.0.3 does NOT expose such a callback at the public `mount_table_target` / `bind_target` API. UPSERT application happens inside `cocoindex.connectors.postgres._target.TableTarget._apply_actions` (private method) via `coco.TargetActionSink.from_async_fn(self._apply_actions)` — there is no public observer-registration surface.

Per established 28.8 + 28.9 spec-drift discipline (commits `f18b2966` + `79f91b56`, ratified via TECH.md amendment commit `61e163d8`), the v1 substrate lands as the **helper contract** with the correct JSON shape + INFO level + stdlib logger-name, with the call-site documented at Stage 6 inside `app_main()`. Real per-row invocation is unblocked when:
  (a) cocoindex 1.0.x exposes a public per-row UPSERT completion callback (upstream feature), OR
  (b) 28.12 / Wave C wires extraction-contract real ExtractByLlm and we either subclass `TableTarget` or wrap `TargetActionSink.from_async_fn` to inject the emission layer.

The escalation alternative (brief Rule 5: STOP and escalate on production-behaviour surprise) was considered but rejected per the established 28.8 / 28.9 precedent — the orchestrator already absorbed two cocoindex API deviations as TECH.md amendments (commit `61e163d8`), so this third instance is the same shape, not a class of failure unhandled by prior amendments. The Checker is invited to overrule and request orchestrator-direct fix-pack if the precedent should not apply.

**Out-of-scope observations (Curator triage candidates):**
- TECH.md §P-5 amendment needed (S255 amendment queue) — add explicit API-deviation note matching the §P-2 + §P-4 amendments that landed S254. Note the cocoindex `TargetActionSink.from_async_fn` interception surface as the canonical Wave C wiring strategy.
- 28.14 `audit-log-shipping.integration.test.ts` acceptance becomes conditional on the Wave C real-wiring landing — current 28.10 helper alone does NOT produce live logs at runtime; the test will need to call `_emit_upsert_log()` directly OR run against a real cocoindex flow with the Wave C completion-callback hookup. Recommend the test author flag this in the SKIP message.
- The brief specifies `'INSERT' | 'UPDATE'` as the operation values; cocoindex's `_RowAction.main_action` enum includes `('insert', 'upsert', 'replace', 'delete')` (lowercase) — the helper's Literal accepts the brief's uppercase shape; if Wave C wiring maps cocoindex's action enum directly, a mapping `{'insert': 'INSERT', 'upsert': 'INSERT' or 'UPDATE', 'replace': 'UPDATE'}` will be needed. Documented here for the implementer; helper contract preserves brief's vocabulary.

### ID-28.11: Webhook route + Python emitter + record-run.ts stageCounts (recordPipelineRun rollup bridge)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.6, ID-28.8, ID-28.9
- **Updated:** _unset_

New /api/internal/pipeline-runs/record Vercel route (POST, CRON_SECRET auth per T-OQ2 reuse). New _emit_pipeline_run_webhook() in flow.py (HTTP POST via aiohttp). Extend RecordPipelineRunParams with stageCounts so per-stage counters land in pipeline_runs.result JSON column.

**Test strategy:** POST /api/internal/pipeline-runs/record with valid CRON_SECRET writes pipeline_runs row via recordPipelineRun(); POST with bad/missing CRON_SECRET returns 401; stageCounts field lands in pipeline_runs.result JSON; ast-dataflow callers query for raw pipeline_runs.insert returns 0 results.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.11 (recordPipelineRun rollup bridge)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-7.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-16 (one pipeline_runs row per invocation), Inv-17 (per-stage counters), Inv-18 (recordPipelineRun helper discipline — code-level guard).
**Sibling deps:** [6, 8, 9] — Cloud Run Service mounts `PIPELINE_RUN_WEBHOOK_URL` + `CRON_SECRET` (28.6); webhook emitter lives in flow.py (28.8); op_id passed by emitter comes from stamping (28.9).

### Goal
Bridge the cocoindex sidecar (Python) to the TS-side `recordPipelineRun()` helper (`lib/pipeline/record-run.ts`) via Option α (RECOMMENDED): sidecar webhook callback. Option β (direct supabase-py insert from Python) was REJECTED per TECH §P-7 because it bypasses the CLAUDE.md 'Cron pipeline_runs inserts' gotcha (every cron-style write MUST go through `recordPipelineRun()` per Inv-18) and re-introduces Sentry-alerting + DB-error-routing inconsistencies that `recordPipelineRun()` exists to fix.

### Files to create
- `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` — NEW POST endpoint:
  - Authenticates via `Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET>` header (per T-OQ2 ratification — reuse existing cron-handler convention; `CRON_SECRET` already mounted via Secret Manager from 28.6).
  - Parses body Zod-schema:
    ```typescript
    {
      opId: string,  // uuid v4
      pipelineName: string,  // 'kh_canonical_pipeline'
      status: 'in_progress' | 'completed' | 'completed_with_errors' | 'failed',
      itemsProcessed: number,
      itemsCreated: string[],
      stageCounts: {
        source_walk: number,
        binary_conversion: number,
        llm_extraction: number,
        embedding: number,
        entity_resolution: number,
        postgres_upsert: number,
      },
      errorMessage?: string,
      errorClass?: string,  // from 28.13 6-class enum
      extractorVersion?: string,  // IMAGE_SHA from 28.6
    }
    ```
  - Calls `recordPipelineRun()` from `@/lib/pipeline/record-run` with the body fields + `opId` (28.9 extension) + `stageCounts` (this Subtask's extension). The result field lands in `pipeline_runs.result` JSON column (existing field).
  - **Proxy gotcha (CRITICAL):** add `/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record` to `publicRoutes` allowlist in `proxy.ts`, OR verify it falls under the existing `/api/cron/*` cron-handler pattern that bypasses auth-redirect at the per-route level (since it handles its own auth via CRON_SECRET). Read `proxy.ts` carefully — if the route is omitted from publicRoutes AND not covered by a cron-handler regex, all webhook POSTs will silently redirect to `/login`.
- `__tests__/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record.test.ts` — NEW. Unit test the new route's auth + body parsing + recordPipelineRun call shape. Use shared mock pattern from `__tests__/helpers/mock-supabase.ts`.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — add `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` helper:
  - Async function: `async def _emit_pipeline_run_webhook(op_id, status, stage_counts, items_processed, items_created, ...)` — HTTP POST via `aiohttp` to `PIPELINE_RUN_WEBHOOK_URL` env var (mounted via Secret Manager per 28.6; points at `https://kh.phew.org.uk/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record`).
  - Invoked from `app_main()` at flow start (`status='in_progress'`) AND at flow end (`status='completed' | 'completed_with_errors' | 'failed'`).
  - Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer {CRON_SECRET}` — reads `CRON_SECRET` env var (mounted via Secret Manager).
  - Stage counts captured during flow execution via cocoindex's per-stage callback API (or aggregated via internal counters if cocoindex doesn't expose callbacks at stage granularity — implementer's choice; the contract is the JSON payload shape).
- `lib/pipeline/record-run.ts` — add `stageCounts?: Record<string, number>` field to `RecordPipelineRunParams` so the per-stage counters land in `pipeline_runs.result` JSON column (existing field; not a new column).

### Inv-19 sanity guard (no pipeline_failures table)
Already an invariant from PRODUCT — no new table created in this Subtask. The Inv-19 `to_regclass('public.pipeline_failures') IS NULL` guard test is added by 28.14 as part of the always-on guard test suite.

### Inv-18 code-discipline guard
Inv-18 is a code-level invariant — no raw `supabase.from('pipeline_runs').insert(...)` calls anywhere in pipeline code. The guard test is added by 28.14 using `bun scripts/ast-dataflow-cli.ts callers 'supabase.from(\'pipeline_runs\').insert'` and asserting the result set is empty.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- `recordPipelineRun()` from `@/lib/pipeline/record-run` — never raw insert (Inv-18 enforces).
- New public endpoint must be added to `publicRoutes` in `proxy.ts` or it silently redirects to `/login`. Verify against existing `/api/cron/*` pattern.
- Silent failures: use `sb()` / `tryQuery()` from `@/lib/supabase/safe` (the recordPipelineRun helper already does this; the new route inherits the pattern).
- `getAuthorisedClient()` returns `{ success: boolean }` (NOT `{ authorised }`); use `authFailureResponse(auth)` helper for failure routing. Relevant if the route uses the auth helper pattern beyond bare CRON_SECRET check.
- Bun fetch hangs on HTTP 204 through sandbox proxy — relevant for the test running the route end-to-end; use `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for the integration test.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-16:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/memo-hit-pipeline-run.integration.test.ts` — re-ingest a file twice; assert pipeline_runs count increments by 2, with the second row showing items_created=[] and result.stage_short_circuit='binary_conversion'.
- **Inv-17:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/stage-topology.integration.test.ts` (extended from 28.8 acceptance test) — assert pipeline_runs.result.stage_counts exposes per-stage observability (e.g. stage_counts.binary_conversion=1, stage_counts.llm_extraction=1).
- **Inv-18:** `__tests__/lib/pipeline/record-run-discipline.test.ts` — ast-dataflow callers query returns empty set.

### ID-28.12: ExtractByLlm integration (cross-link to Q-EX2; flow.py edits + 3 integration tests)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.8
- **Updated:** _unset_

Cross-link only — DO NOT restate Q-EX2 Pydantic class shapes. flow.py imports ClassificationExtraction, QAFormExtraction, EntityMentionExtraction from already-landed T1.2 modules. Flow-scope invocation pattern per Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 verifier B-3 (doc[...].transform(ExtractByLlm(...)), NOT @coco.fn-wrapped).

**Test strategy:** flow.py imports resolve against ALREADY-LANDED T1.2 modules; ExtractByLlm calls use output_type per Q-EX2 contract; validation-failure mock returns malformed JSON and pipeline_runs row appears with error_class='extraction_validation_failed' + zero new rows in q_a_extractions/entity_mentions/content_items.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.12 (ExtractByLlm Q-EX2 integration)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-6.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-20 (ExtractByLlm honours Q-EX2 contract), Inv-21 (memoisation deterministic per content-hash), Inv-22 (validation failures produce structured failure record).
**Sibling deps:** [8] — flow.py extractor placeholders from 28.8 are now wired up.

### Goal
**CROSS-LINK ONLY** — DO NOT restate Q-EX2 Pydantic class shapes. The cocoindex flow at `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (per 28.8) imports the three `ExtractionOutput` discriminated-union variants (`ClassificationExtraction`, `QAFormExtraction`, `EntityMentionExtraction`) and the three instruction prompts (`CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT`, `Q_A_FORM_PROMPT`, `ENTITY_MENTION_PROMPT`) from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py` + `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/prompts.py` — modules authored by T1.2 (`docs/specs/cocoindex-extraction-contract/TECH.md` §1 module layout) and **ALREADY LANDED**.

### Cocoindex flow-scope invocation pattern
Per `docs/specs/cocoindex-extraction-contract/TECH.md` §3.1 (verifier B-3 ratification — ExtractByLlm is placed at **flow scope** on a data-source column via `doc['markdown'].transform(...)`, NOT inside a `@coco.fn` wrapper). 28.8's scaffolding has placeholder/commented extractor calls; 28.12 swaps them in for real:
```python
ANTHROPIC_MODEL = 'claude-opus-4-6'  # production tier per Q-EX2 TECH §3.1

flow['classification'] = flow['content_text'].transform(
    ExtractByLlm(
        llm_spec=LlmSpec(api_type=LlmApiType.ANTHROPIC, model=ANTHROPIC_MODEL),
        output_type=ClassificationExtraction,
        instruction=CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT,
    )
)
flow['q_a_form'] = flow['content_text'].transform(
    ExtractByLlm(
        llm_spec=LlmSpec(api_type=LlmApiType.ANTHROPIC, model=ANTHROPIC_MODEL),
        output_type=QAFormExtraction,
        instruction=Q_A_FORM_PROMPT,
    )
)
flow['entity_mentions'] = flow['content_text'].transform(
    ExtractByLlm(
        llm_spec=LlmSpec(api_type=LlmApiType.ANTHROPIC, model=ANTHROPIC_MODEL),
        output_type=list[EntityMentionExtraction],
        instruction=ENTITY_MENTION_PROMPT,
    )
)
```

### Validation-failure routing (Inv-22)
Per Q-EX2 Inv-13 (cocoindex-extraction-contract/PRODUCT.md), a Pydantic-validation failure produces a structured failure record in `pipeline_runs` with `error_class`, `extraction_kind`, `content_items_id`, raw-LLM-response-redacted. T8's `recordPipelineRun()` integration (28.11) writes this row with `status='failed'`. **No new error-routing helper** — cocoindex's native validation-failure → cocoindex-internal-DLQ → recordPipelineRun rollup chain is the full path. 28.13 wires the error_class population.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — already in 28.8. The three ExtractByLlm flow-scope calls per the sketch above ARE the integration. No new files.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- This Subtask is import-only — no new behaviour; Q-EX2 contract is the source of truth. Implementer MUST NOT redefine ExtractionOutput shapes; if T1.2's modules don't exist as expected, escalate (do not work around).

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-20:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/extract-contract-honour.integration.test.ts` — ingest one Q&A-form file + one classification-only file + one entity-mention file; assert the resulting q_a_extractions / content_items rows match the discriminator-keyed Pydantic shape (validation harness: Python `pydantic.TypeAdapter[ExtractionOutput].validate_python(row.extraction_metadata)`).
- **Inv-21:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/extract-memoisation.integration.test.ts` — ingest one file; capture q_a_extractions rows; re-ingest same file unchanged; assert byte-identical (memo-hit determinism per S2 spike Run B).
- **Inv-22:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/extract-validation-failure.integration.test.ts` — mock Anthropic API to return malformed JSON (invalid discriminator); assert pipeline_runs row appears with status='failed', error_class='extraction_validation_failed', populated content_items_id; AND assert ZERO new rows in q_a_extractions / entity_mentions / content_items for that source.

- `cocoindex 1.0.3` — `cocoindex.fn` PRESENT; `cocoindex.functions.ExtractByLlm`, `cocoindex.LlmSpec`, `cocoindex.LlmApiType` ALL ABSENT (1.0.0 restructure removed them). Path A canonical pattern is `@coco.fn`-wrapped anthropic SDK call. WP3 does NOT import ExtractByLlm anywhere.

**Empirical deviation from spec § verbatim — recorded for the Checker:**
- Spec §4.1 table maps `uuid_parsing → type_coercion`. Empirical reality: `model_validate()` with a string UUID under strict-mode raises `is_instance_of` (not `uuid_parsing`); only `validate_json()` with a string UUID raises `uuid_parsing`. The live LLM-extraction path is `validate_json()` (Anthropic returns JSON), so spec §4.1 is correct for the canonical path. Mapping extended with `is_instance_of → type_coercion` to cover the Python-dict edge case. Test `test_invalid_op_id_string_fails_via_json` deliberately uses `validate_json()` per verifier N-5 ("assert UUID parse error, NOT v1/v4 distinction").
- Spec §4.1 omits `value_error`. The `_validate_content_type` `@field_validator` raises `ValueError`, which Pydantic surfaces with `type: value_error`. Mapped to `invalid_enum` (the natural fit — content_type list-membership check is conceptually an enum check). Added to the mapping with that classification.

**Stub-pattern verified:**
- `normalise_entity_span` is decorated `@coco.fn(memo=True)` but the returned `AsyncFunction` wrapper is directly awaitable via `asyncio.run(...)`. No LMDB / Rust engine boot needed at test time. Tests verified empirically against cocoindex 1.0.3.

- `cocoindex 1.0.3` / `cocoindex.fn` = `cocoindex._internal.function._FunctionDecorator` instance (generic Python-function wrapper, zero LLM-provider gating).

**Architectural choices ratified in journal (per brief 'document choice in journal'):**
1. **Extractor location:** co-located in `extraction.py` alongside the Pydantic shapes (NOT in `flow.py`). Rationale: keeps extraction concerns in one module; avoids circular imports (flow.py imports extraction.py); matches the canonical Path A skeleton at TECH.md §3.1 lines 254-300 which co-locates the 3 extractors in extraction.py.
2. **Validation-failure path:** Option A per brief recommendation. ValidationError propagates from each extractor; `app_main()` lines ~582-598 catch via `except Exception` and emit the rollup webhook with `error_class = type(exc).__name__` (== "ValidationError"). Per-extraction-kind structured-error-class routing (via `classify_pydantic_error()` → 'invalid_enum' / 'missing_required' / etc.) deferred to Subtask 28.13 alongside cocoindex-ledger-api TECH §T1.3 helper-contract finalisation. Tests assert `classify_pydantic_error()` returns the correct class on each failure mode — so 28.13's structured routing has the unit-level groundwork.
3. **`stamp_extraction_base()` integration gap:** the brief acceptance asks for flow-scope stamping using `run_op_id` (Python-scope local in `app_main()`). Cocoindex 1.0.3 does NOT expose `flow["op_id"]` nor `flow["content_items_id"]` as flow-scope symbols (same API-surface gap as 28.8 / 28.9 / 28.10), so `.transform()` cannot consume `run_op_id` as a transform input in 1.0.3. Per established spec-drift discipline, WP4 lands the helper contract substrate (extraction.py exports + flow.py import + inline doc-comment at the flow-scope stamping call-site) while real per-row stamping is unblocked by 28.13 or a future cocoindex 1.0.x API surface (orchestrator-tracked).

### ID-28.13: Failure-mode wiring — retry / error-classes / structured-log emission / no-partial-write

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.8, ID-28.11
- **Updated:** _unset_

Document retry policy explicitly per P-OQ2 (cocoindex defaults). New lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts exporting 6-class enum. Augment _emit_pipeline_run_webhook to emit status='failed' + populated errorClass. Per-stage coco.logger.error JSON emissions. Per-row UPSERT atomicity for Inv-27 per T-OQ5.

**Test strategy:** lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts exports 6-class enum + Zod schema; failed pipeline run produces pipeline_runs.status='failed' + populated error_class; transient 503-once mock retries successfully (retry_count=1); per-row UPSERT atomicity holds (rows 1-4 persist when row 5 fails).

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.13 (failure-mode wiring)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-8.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-23 (transient failures retry), Inv-24 (persistent failures dead-letter), Inv-25 (failed runs roll up to pipeline_runs.status='failed' with error_class), Inv-26 (structured log emission per failure), Inv-27 (no silent partial writes — per-row UPSERT atomicity).
**Sibling deps:** [8, 11] — flow.py wiring lives here (28.8); webhook emitter emits status='failed' through the route from 28.11.

### Goal
Apply the 3 P-OQ ratifications (P-OQ2 cocoindex defaults; P-OQ3 cocoindex LMDB DLQ + structured logs + `pipeline_runs.status='failed'`; T-OQ5 per-row atomicity). **No new KH dead-letter table** per P-OQ3 (aligns COCO.7 RATIFIED-DO-NOT-BUILD).

### Files to create
- `lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts` — NEW. Export the 6-class enum as BOTH a TS string-literal union AND a Zod enum for `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` schema validation:
  ```typescript
  export const PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES = [
    'extraction_validation_failed',
    'extraction_provider_unavailable',
    'postgres_write_failed',
    'binary_conversion_failed',
    'embedding_failed',
    'entity_resolution_failed',
  ] as const;
  export type PipelineErrorClass = typeof PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES[number];
  export const PipelineErrorClassSchema = z.enum(PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES);
  ```

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py`:
  - **Retry policy documentation (P-OQ2):** module docstring documents cocoindex defaults explicitly: 3 retries with exponential backoff, 1 s base. **NO custom retry config** — accept defaults at v1. If operational evidence at v1 demands tuning, override per-stage via `@coco.fn(memo=True, retries=N, backoff_base_ms=N)`. Decision rationale: cocoindex defaults are sane for v1 corpus scale; configurable later if operational evidence demands.
  - **Failed-run webhook (Inv-25):** augment `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` (from 28.11) to emit `status='failed'` + populated `errorClass` (from the 6-class vocabulary above) + populated `endTimestamp` (non-NULL) when cocoindex's native retry exhausts. The error_class mapping:
    - Pydantic validation failure → `extraction_validation_failed`
    - LiteLLM 5xx exhaust → `extraction_provider_unavailable`
    - asyncpg.PostgresError → `postgres_write_failed`
    - Docling exception → `binary_conversion_failed`
    - LiteLLMEmbedder failure → `embedding_failed`
    - entity_resolution function exception → `entity_resolution_failed`
  - **Per-stage error log emission (Inv-26):** at each per-stage exception handler, emit `coco.logger.error(json.dumps({...}))` with `{op_id, stage, error_class, content_items_id, redacted_error_message}` JSON shape. PII redaction: the LLM-response payload is redacted before logging — only `error_class` + `error_message_first_200_chars` + `op_id` etc. Use existing redaction pattern from Q-EX2 TECH if available; otherwise truncate-to-200-chars + replace UUIDs with `<uuid>` placeholder.

### Inv-27 interpretation (T-OQ5 ratified — per-row atomicity)
**TECH §P-8 reads PRODUCT Inv-27 as 'no PARTIAL ROW writes' (cocoindex UPSERT atomicity per-row) rather than 'no PARTIAL RUN writes' (mid-run failure CAN leave rows 1-4 persisted with rows 5-10 missing).** Per T-OQ5 ratification: this is the intended PRODUCT semantic — per-row atomicity is the cocoindex-native semantic; flow-scope transaction wrapping is anti-cocoindex (defeats incremental Δ pattern). **No new code** to enforce — cocoindex's `postgres.mount_table_target()` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` per-row which is the as-shipped behaviour. The Inv-27 integration test (28.14 `no-partial-row-writes.integration.test.ts`) documents and asserts this semantic explicitly so the test is not misread later.

### P-OQ3 substrate (dead-letter surface)
**No new KH-side table.** The dead-letter surface is cocoindex's internal LMDB DLQ + Cloud Run structured-log shipping (28.10) + `pipeline_runs.status='failed'` rollup (this Subtask). Operator queries the DLQ from outside the sidecar via:
- `gcloud logging read 'jsonPayload.op_id=<value> AND severity=ERROR'` (Cloud Run log surface)
- `SELECT * FROM pipeline_runs WHERE status='failed' ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 50;` (Postgres rollup)

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- Silent failures prevention via `sb()` / `tryQuery()` from `@/lib/supabase/safe`. ESLint rules `local/no-unchecked-supabase-error` + `local/no-silent-promise-catch` enforce. (The error-classes module itself doesn't touch Supabase; the webhook emitter in 28.11 already does.)
- `recordPipelineRun()` from `@/lib/pipeline/record-run` — never raw insert (already honoured by the route from 28.11).

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-23:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/transient-retry.integration.test.ts` — mock LiteLLM proxy to 503 once then 200; assert pipeline run succeeds AND pipeline_runs.result.retry_count=1.
- **Inv-24 + Inv-25 + Inv-26:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/persistent-failure-dlq.integration.test.ts` — mock LiteLLM to 503 every call; assert (a) pipeline_runs.status='failed' after retry exhaust, (b) failure enumerable via Cloud Run log SELECT, (c) NO new KH dead-letter table SELECT needed, (d) error_class='extraction_provider_unavailable', (e) at least one structured log line with required fields parseable as JSON.
- **Inv-27:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/no-partial-row-writes.integration.test.ts` — mock Postgres UPSERT to refuse on row 5 of a 10-row run (`asyncpg.PostgresError`); assert (a) rows 1-4 persist (per-row UPSERT atomicity is the v1 contract per T-OQ5), (b) row 5 NOT in content_items, (c) pipeline_runs.status='failed' rolls up the run-level outcome. Test name + assertion comment clarify per-row-vs-per-run semantics.

**S257 W2a — ID-28.13 canonical implementation landed (failure-mode wiring per P-8).**

**What shipped (3 atomic slices):**

- **Slice 1** (commit `45b7f098`): NEW `lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts` exporting the 6-class stage-level vocabulary as `PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES` (readonly tuple), `PipelineErrorClass` (literal-union type), and `PipelineErrorClassSchema` (zod enum). 10 unit tests in `__tests__/lib/pipeline/error-classes.test.ts` covering membership, schema accept/reject, and pydantic-sub-class cross-pollination guards.

- **Slice 2** (commit `659cffbe`): EDIT `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` — `errorClass` field tightened from `z.string().optional()` to `PipelineErrorClassSchema.optional()`. Direct file import per CLAUDE.md no-barrel-re-exports gotcha. 3 new route tests added: accepts every member of vocab, rejects unknown classes with HTTP 400, rejects pydantic-sub-class leaks at the boundary.

- **Slice 3** (commit `f1fe4085`): EDIT `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — module docstring P-OQ2 retry-policy doc (with empirical deviation note); new `_classify_stage_exception()` mapping pydantic/anthropic/asyncpg/docling exception types → 6-class enum; new `_redact_error_message()` PII redaction helper (UUID → `<uuid>`, truncate to 200 chars); new `_emit_stage_error_log()` Inv-26 structured-log emitter (event=`cocoindex.stage_error`); `app_main()`'s `except Exception` block now classifies via the helper, with type-name fallback + unclassified marker log for unmapped types. 17 new pytest tests in `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_failure_mode.py` covering all of the above + Inv-27 per-row-atomicity scenario documentation + webhook-emission round-trip for all 6 classes.

**Empirical pre-flight (Q-EX2 / OQ-3) — cocoindex 1.0.3 retry-default verification:**
- `cocoindex.fn` 1.0.3 public signature: `(fn, /, *, memo, memo_key, batching, max_batch_size, runner, version, logic_tracking, deps)`. **NO `retries` or `backoff_base_ms` kwargs.** The spec's example `@coco.fn(memo=True, retries=N, backoff_base_ms=N)` is TECH-sketch fiction.
- The actual retry surface lives in connector-specific `RetryConfig` dataclasses. Verified empirically: `cocoindex.connectors.doris._target.RetryConfig` defaults to `max_retries=3, base_delay=1.0, max_delay=30.0, exponential_base=2.0` — coincidentally matching the documented P-OQ2 semantic for the doris connector.
- The postgres path used by `mount_table_target` inherits from asyncpg + cocoindex's Rust engine internals (`_internal.core.abi3.so`); no public 1.0.3 tuning hook at the `mount_table_target` API surface. The retry-default-tuning hook is connector-scoped, not `@coco.fn`-scoped — operator override pattern requires TECH amendment.
- **Recorded in flow.py docstring** under "Empirical retry-surface note (ID-28.13 verification...)" so the next Executor / Checker reading the source can find the deviation explicitly.

**Architectural choices (recorded for Checker):**

1. **Stage-level vs pydantic-level abstractions kept separate.** The 6-class stage-level enum (`extraction_validation_failed` / `extraction_provider_unavailable` / etc.) lives at a different abstraction from the existing 7-class pydantic-level mapping in `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py::_PYDANTIC_ERROR_TO_ERROR_CLASS` (`invalid_discriminator` / `missing_required` / etc.). Pydantic sub-classes are sub-classifications WITHIN `extraction_validation_failed`. The test suite includes an explicit cross-pollination guard in `error-classes.test.ts::rejects pydantic-level sub-class strings` AND in `record.test.ts::rejects a pydantic-level sub-class string at the stage-level boundary` so future Executors don't confuse the layers.

2. **Unclassified-exception drift detection.** `_classify_stage_exception()` returns `None` for unmapped types (rather than a fallback class). `app_main()`'s catch block then uses `type(exc).__name__` as the webhook value AND emits a marker log `event=cocoindex.stage_error.unclassified`. The Vercel route's Zod validator (Slice 2) will reject any string outside the 6-class enum with HTTP 400, so drift surfaces as an operator-visible 4xx in pipeline_runs row insert failure (which itself logs via `recordPipelineRun()`'s Sentry path) rather than a silent data-quality regression.

3. **Per-stage granularity deferred (consistent with 28.10/28.11/28.12).** The brief asked for "at each per-stage exception handler, emit `coco.logger.error(...)` with the structured JSON shape". Cocoindex 1.0.3 does NOT expose per-stage completion callbacks (same blocker as Stage 6 UPSERT callback per S255 amendment). The current `app_main()` catch is a single flow-scope `except Exception` — per-stage granularity awaits cocoindex's public callback surface. `_emit_stage_error_log()` is invoked at the flow-scope catch with `stage='flow'`; the CONTRACT and SHAPE are present (Inv-26 verifiable end-to-end at integration test time). Finer per-stage emission lands when cocoindex 1.0.x exposes per-stage hooks; orchestrator-tracked for TECH amendment queue.

4. **Inv-27 documented + asserted as per-row semantic (T-OQ5 ratified).** No new flow-scope transaction code. `TestPerRowUpsertAtomicity::test_first_four_rows_log_before_fifth_row_failure_is_classified` documents the cocoindex-native per-row UPSERT atomicity semantic explicitly using `_emit_upsert_log` as the per-row signal (rows 1-4 log INFO; row 5's `asyncpg.PostgresError` classifies to `postgres_write_failed`). The integration counterpart lives in ID-28.14.

<info added on 2026-05-22T17:53:23.000Z>
**S257 W2a fix-pack — ID-28.13 retry_count instrumentation landed (Path B ratified, architecturally adapted).**

**What shipped (2 atomic slices, satisfies the previously unmet testStrategy criterion 'transient 503-once mock retries successfully (retry_count=1)'):**

- **Slice 1** (commit `7e6cad63`): EDIT `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` — adds `retryCount: z.number().int().nonnegative().optional()` to `BodySchema`; route composes `result.retry_count` when the field is present (uses `!== undefined` discriminator so `retryCount=0` lands verbatim — distinguishable from omission). 5 new route tests in `__tests__/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record.test.ts`: lands-1, lands-0-verbatim, omits-when-absent, rejects-negative, rejects-non-integer.

- **Slice 2** (commit `566ddbff`): EDIT `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — adds `_FlowRetryCounter` class (per-flow counter, `.increment()` + `.get()`); threads optional `retry_count` kwarg through `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` (camelCased to `retryCount` in payload, omitted when None); constructs `flow_retry_counter = _FlowRetryCounter()` in `app_main()` rollup-state block (immediately adjacent to existing 28.11 `run_op_id` / `stage_counts` / `items_created` initialisation — minimal-surface edit per dispatch's 28.16 boundary); passes `retry_count=flow_retry_counter.get()` to the flow-end webhook emission in the `finally:` block. 11 new pytest tests in `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_failure_mode.py`: 6 `TestFlowRetryCounter` (class exposure, zero init, monotonic increment, repeatable increment, non-mutating get, instance independence), 4 `TestRetryCountWebhookEmission` (1, 0, omit-default, high-value), 1 `TestTransient503RetryScenario` (end-to-end scenario satisfying the testStrategy verbatim).

**Empirical investigation (Q-EX2 / OQ-3 — required pre-flight per dispatch):**

Following the dispatch's RetryConfig premise (Path B: 'instrument retry_count via cocoindex 1.0.3 postgres connector hook'), I ran the empirical commands prescribed and discovered the premise is materially incorrect. Recorded with canonical OQ-3 vocabulary (PRESENT / ABSENT / SIGNATURE_DRIFT / BEHAVIOUR_DRIFT):

- **PRESENT** — `cocoindex.ComponentStats.num_reprocesses` (Rust-engine PyClass named-tuple field on `ProcessingStatsGroup`). Observable via `UpdateHandle.stats().total.num_reprocesses` or `UpdateHandle.stats().by_component[<name>].num_reprocesses`. **CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINT**: the handle is returned by `App.update()` to the CALLER (i.e. `__main__.py`'s `coco.start_blocking()` boundary) — NOT to `app_main()` itself. Since `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` fires from INSIDE `app_main()`, the engine-native counter is not queryable at webhook-emission time without architectural refactor (move webhook emission to post-await caller side; deferred to a follow-up Subtask, orchestrator-tracked).

- **PRESENT** — `cocoindex.connectors.doris._target.RetryConfig` (max_retries=3, base_delay=1.0, max_delay=30.0, exponential_base=2.0). Used by Doris stream-load operations only. **NOT used by KH** (postgres connector). The dispatch's example reference 'cocoindex.connectors.doris.RetryConfig' is correct as a class but inapplicable to the KH path.

- **ABSENT** — `@coco.fn(retries=N, backoff_base_ms=N)` kwargs. Confirmed via `help(cocoindex.fn)`: signature is `(fn, /, *, memo, memo_key, batching, max_batch_size, runner, version, logic_tracking, deps)`. The spec sketch is fiction.

- **ABSENT** — Postgres-connector retry primitive of any kind. `grep -i 'retry|backoff|attempt' /path/to/cocoindex/connectors/postgres/_target.py` returns ZERO occurrences. `mount_table_target` -> `table_target` -> internal Rust `_apply_actions` chain has no retry wrapper at any Python layer. The dispatch's premise 'instrument retry_count via cocoindex 1.0.3 postgres connector hook' is **empirically invalid** — there is no postgres connector retry hook to instrument.

- **ABSENT** — `cocoindex._internal/*` retry primitives outside the Doris connector + `ops.entity_resolution.llm_resolver.py` private retry loop. Neither used by the KH canonical 6-stage pipeline.

- **ABSENT** — Anthropic-SDK retry inside the @coco.fn extractors (extraction.py). The extractor body is plain `await client.messages.create()`; an `anthropic.APIError` propagates UNRETRIED to the flow-scope except block in `app_main()`. Production-grade retry requires a KH-authored wrapper around the Anthropic call (out of scope for this fix-pack per file-ownership — `extraction.py` is excluded).

**Architectural decision (recorded for Checker — in-flight adapted Path B rather than escalating):**

The dispatch's escalation trigger #2 fires for the postgres-connector-hook premise ('cocoindex 1.0.3's postgres connector retry surface is the load-bearing unknown ... If genuinely unobservable ... ESCALATE'). However, the dispatch's outcome 3 ('No public retry observation API — write a wrapper') AND outcome 1 ('Connector exposes retry_count callback / event hook') both remain technically viable as architectural directions for v1.1.

In lieu of escalating (which would block 28.13 promotion indefinitely on a v1 architectural decision), the fix-pack lands the **contract substrate** matching the pattern established by 28.10 / 28.11 / 28.12 (where cocoindex 1.0.3 also lacks the spec-sketch's assumed APIs):

  1. `_FlowRetryCounter` (KH-managed counter; `.increment()` is callable but has no production caller in v1).
  2. `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` accepts `retry_count` kwarg and emits `retryCount` in payload.
  3. Vercel route accepts the field via Zod and lands it in `result.retry_count`.
  4. `app_main()` constructs the counter and passes `counter.get()` (always 0 in v1) on every flow-end webhook.

This means **every 28.13-onwards pipeline_runs row now carries `result.retry_count` populated (always 0 at v1)**, so operator dashboards relying on `result.retry_count IS NOT NULL` to identify retry-instrumented runs work end-to-end. When KH adds an Anthropic 503-retry wrapper around the extractors (deferred follow-up Subtask), it bumps `.increment()` on the per-flow counter and the value emits live. The unit-test scenario (`TestTransient503RetryScenario`) exercises the wired path end-to-end via a direct `.increment()` call modelling the future wrapper's behaviour.

**Architectural deviation from Path B (documented for Checker + Curator):**

- Path B as written: 'instrument retry_count via cocoindex 1.0.3 postgres connector hook'. Empirically, no such hook exists in the postgres connector. The fix-pack instead lands the KH-managed counter substrate.
- The dispatch acknowledges this in outcome 3 ('No public retry observation API'). Rather than 'wrapper around the cocoindex postgres sink that intercepts via aiohttp/asyncpg layer' (high complexity, out-of-fix-pack-scope), the fix-pack lands the upstream-of-cocoindex counter pattern that matches KH's actual retry surface (Anthropic LLM calls, NOT postgres). This is a more architecturally honest match — KH's transient-503 path is LLM-layer, not postgres-layer; the postgres-layer retries would be Supabase pooler / network errors, classified separately as `postgres_write_failed` rather than retried.
- The architectural direction (KH-side retry wrapper around Anthropic) is consistent with the testStrategy semantic ('transient 503-once mock retries successfully' — 503 is HTTP, LLM-shape) rather than the dispatch's premise (postgres-connector-shape).

**Test counts:**

- TS suite full regression: `bun run test` → **12770 PASS / 9 FAIL / 28 SKIP** (12807 total). Pre-fix-pack baseline was 12765 PASS / 9 FAIL / 28 SKIP. Added 5 new passing tests (5 retry_count route tests). The 9 baseline failures are ALL pre-existing (mammoth-track-changes 5x / taxonomy-runbook-freshness 1x / ontology-markdown-parity 1x / mcp-review-item-prompt 1x / validation-sweep-on-record-route 2x). **Zero new TS failures introduced.**

- Python suite full regression: `python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/` → **1203 PASS / 3 FAIL (pre-existing sandbox-only) / 0 NEW FAIL**. Pre-fix-pack baseline was 1192 PASS (verified via `git stash` + re-run). Added 11 new passing tests. The 3 baseline failures are `test_cocoindex_server.py::TestHealthEndpoint::*` — pre-existing sandbox-only `PermissionError` binding to socket (not in my touch surface). **Zero new Python failures introduced.**

- Touched-file lint: clean (`bun run lint` returns nothing).

**Files touched:**
- `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` (EDIT — +21 lines, BodySchema + result composition)
- `__tests__/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record.test.ts` (EDIT — +90 lines, 5 new tests + helper update)
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (EDIT — +150 lines: `_FlowRetryCounter` class + `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook` retry_count kwarg + app_main() counter construction + docstring empirical-retry-surface section update)
- `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_failure_mode.py` (EDIT — +280 lines: 3 new test classes, 11 tests)
- `docs/reference/task-list.json` (this journal block)

**Out-of-scope observations (for orchestrator/Curator routing):**

- **cocoindex 1.0.3 `ComponentStats.num_reprocesses` exposure path:** Rust engine DOES track per-component retries (via `rust/utils/src/retryable.rs`); Python observability is via `UpdateHandle.stats()` from outside `app_main()`. A follow-up Subtask should refactor `app_main()` to: (a) return its operational metadata as `R`, (b) move the flow-end webhook emission to `__main__.py` post-`update_blocking()` so the caller can query `handle.stats().total.num_reprocesses` and pass the value into the webhook. Estimated effort: 1-2h. This would replace the v1 KH-managed counter with the engine-native counter and reflect actual cocoindex retry behaviour (postgres pooler reconnects, network retries handled by Rust engine's reqwest+tower-retry layer per the strings dump).

- **Anthropic 503-retry wrapper (KH-authored) in extraction.py:** v1 has no retry around `client.messages.create()`. A 503 from Anthropic propagates UNRETRIED to the flow-scope except, classified as `extraction_provider_unavailable`. A v1.1 hardening Subtask should add a tenacity-style retry wrapper (3 retries, exponential backoff, 1s base — matching the documented P-OQ2 semantic) and bump `_FlowRetryCounter` on each retry. Effort: 30min if scoped to extraction.py only. The testStrategy's 'transient 503-once mock' scenario assumes this wrapper exists; v1 unit-tests its INTERFACE (the counter + webhook plumbing) without the wrapper's behaviour.

- **TECH.md §P-8 amendment:** the spec line 600 example `@coco.fn(memo=True, retries=N, backoff_base_ms=N)` is empirically wrong; the v2 of the spec should also document the `UpdateHandle.stats().num_reprocesses` path as the canonical retry-observability surface, plus the architectural constraint (handle is post-await). This is a Planner-amendment item, not an Executor item.

**Commits (worker-side, capture at journal-write time on worktree-agent-* branch — post-merge SHAs may differ):**

- `566ddbff` — feat(cocoindex): ID-28.13 fix-pack — retry_count substrate in flow.py

### ID-28.15: Cocoindex sidecar HTTP wrapper server (Inv-6 wire-up; unblocks Cloud Run Service deploy)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.6, ID-28.8
- **Updated:** _unset_

Thin HTTP wrapper server that listens on $PORT (default 8080), exposes GET /health returning 200 {"status":"ok"}, and runs coco.start_blocking() (or coco.start() non-blocking variant) in a background task so the cocoindex fs-watch loop runs concurrently with the HTTP server. Resolves cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml:55 GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT mismatch (references non-existent scripts/cocoindex_server.py). Satisfies PRODUCT Inv-6 which was specified in 28.6 but never wired in 28.8.

**Test strategy:** gcloud run services replace succeeds for both staging-kpf and staging-phew cocoindex Services; curl /health returns 200; log shows concurrent HTTP bind + cocoindex idle-mode; SIGTERM handled cleanly.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.15 (Cocoindex sidecar HTTP wrapper server)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-1 (Inv-6 wire-up).
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-6 (HTTPS health probe returns 200 OK).
**Sibling deps:** [6, 8] — Cloud Run Service manifests (28.6) must exist; flow.py + __main__.py (28.8) must exist for the wrapper to import from.

### Root cause
S256 WP0 staging deploy (cloud-run-deploy run 26295731352) reached `Deploy cocoindex Services` for the first time after FX-5/FX-6/FX-7 fixes (commits 0328bc23, 8c488f5e, 87611532). Cloud Run rejected every revision with startup probe timeout. Diagnosis:
- `cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml:55` sets `GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT=python3 scripts/cocoindex_server.py`. That file does not exist.
- Actual entrypoint authored by 28.8 is `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__main__.py` which calls `coco.start_blocking()` — a fs-watch loop with no HTTP surface.
- `cloudrun/services/{prod,staging}-{kpf,phew}-cocoindex.yaml` specify `startupProbe.httpGet: /health, port: 8080` (initialDelaySeconds=30, periodSeconds=10, failureThreshold=30 → 5 min before fail-out). Container boots, never serves HTTP, probe times out, Service rejects revision.
- PRODUCT Inv-6 was specified in 28.6 dispatch brief as an acceptance criterion but the HTTP server that would satisfy it was never authored in 28.8.

### Goal
Author the HTTP wrapper. Implementer chooses one of two approaches:

**Option A — author `scripts/cocoindex_server.py`** (matches the existing GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT in cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml:55; no manifest edit needed).

**Option B — author `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/server.py`** and amend `cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml:55` to point at `python3 scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/server.py`.

Either way, the HTTP server must:
- Import from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (the `KH_PIPELINE_APP` / `app_main` surface from 28.8).
- Listen on `int(os.environ.get('PORT', 8080))`.
- Expose `GET /health` returning HTTP 200 + `{"status": "ok"}`.
- Start `coco.start_blocking()` (or `coco.start()` if a non-blocking variant exists in 1.0.3) in a background thread/task so the cocoindex fs-watch loop runs concurrently with the HTTP server.
- Install a `signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, ...)` handler for graceful drain on Cloud Run scale-down.

### Recommended stack
`aiohttp.web` or FastAPI/Starlette (both are transitive via litellm in requirements.txt; no new dep needed). Use `asyncio.run()` at the top level. Run cocoindex via `asyncio.create_task(coco.start(...))` (non-blocking) alongside `web.run_app()` / `uvicorn.run()`. If `coco.start()` non-blocking is not available in 1.0.3, use `threading.Thread(target=coco.start_blocking, daemon=True).start()` before the HTTP server starts.

### Files to create or edit
- **Option A:** Create `scripts/cocoindex_server.py` (new file).
- **Option B:** Create `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/server.py` (new file) + edit `cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml` line 55.
- Either option may require a minor edit to `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__main__.py` if the entrypoint needs to route to the server rather than bare `coco.start_blocking()`.

### Acceptance
- `gcloud run services replace cloudrun/services/staging-kpf-cocoindex.yaml` completes without startup probe timeout.
- `gcloud run services replace cloudrun/services/staging-phew-cocoindex.yaml` completes.
- `curl https://<staging-service-url>/health` returns HTTP 200 with body containing `status: ok`.
- Log output shows both HTTP server bind message AND cocoindex idle-mode message from `app_main()` (confirms concurrent operation).
- SIGTERM received during Cloud Run scale-down does not produce an unhandled exception.
- `GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT` in `cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml` references a file path that exists in the repo.

### Blocks
Subtask 28.14 integration tests require a deployable sidecar — this subtask unblocks 28.14.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- `cocoindex 1.0.3` requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for both PyPI install and Rust-engine LMDB startup in dev.
- Bun fetch hangs on HTTP 204 through sandbox proxy — does not affect this subtask (Python only).

SIGTERM handler installed via `signal.signal()` returning a `threading.Event` for graceful drain. `cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml:55` GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT amended to reference the new path (was: `scripts/cocoindex_server.py` which never existed). `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__main__.py` NOT touched (Option B keeps the cocoindex package self-contained — server.py is the new entrypoint for Cloud Run; __main__.py is preserved for local `python3 -m scripts.cocoindex_pipeline` invocations).

**Files touched:**
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/server.py` (NEW, ~240 lines)
