**Spec slice:** PRODUCT.md §Inv-6 (HTTPS /health probe returns 200 OK) — wired by this Subtask. TECH.md §P-1 (Cloud Run Service deploy contract).

**Framework choice:** aiohttp 3.13.5 (already transitive via litellm — no new dep). Empirical-import-and-call check (Q-EX2 / OQ-3 discipline): `python3 -c "import aiohttp; print(aiohttp.__version__)"` returned `3.13.5`; FastAPI/Starlette/Flask absent. cocoindex 1.0.3 verified to expose `start_blocking`, `start`, `App` (all three confirmed present).

**Tests:** 9 unit tests pass in isolation. Full Python regression CLEAN: `1178 passed, 11 warnings in 33.10s` (all 145 cocoindex-suite tests + 1033 other Python tests pass).

**Local smoke test (Acceptance §3-§4):** `PORT=8086 python3 _smoke_server.py &` with coco.start_blocking patched to sleep (no Rust engine boot in dev). `curl localhost:8086/health` returned: HTTP 200, Content-Type `application/json; charset=utf-8`, body `{"status": "ok"}`. Log output showed BOTH `Starting cocoindex sidecar HTTP wrapper on port=8086` AND `cocoindex background thread starting via coco.start_blocking()` — confirms concurrent operation. SIGTERM (kill -TERM) delivered cleanly with no traceback after shutdown. Smoke-test launcher cleaned up post-run.

**Acceptance criteria coverage:**
- ✓ HTTP server binds on $PORT (default 8080) — `resolve_port()` + 3 unit tests covering default, override, ValueError on malformed.
- ✓ GET /health → 200 + JSON `{"status": "ok"}` — 3 unit tests + smoke verified.
- ✓ coco.start_blocking() in background daemon thread — `start_cocoindex_thread()` + unit test asserts `daemon=True` + mock-call verification.
- ✓ SIGTERM handler installed — 2 unit tests (handler replaces SIG_DFL + sets a `threading.Event` drain flag).
- ✓ `from scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.flow import KH_PIPELINE_APP` still loads cleanly post-edit — direct import verified + WP4 idle-mode test (8/8) still passes in isolation.
- ✓ GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT references file that exists in repo — `cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml:60` now points at `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/server.py`.
- ⊘ DEFERRED to deploy-time: `gcloud run services replace` succeeds, log shows concurrent operation in production — these acceptance lines require Cloud Run deploy invocation (orchestrator owns post-merge).

**Deviations from brief:**
1. `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__main__.py` was NOT touched. The brief listed it as OPTIONAL minor edit. Option B keeps `__main__.py` as the local-dev entrypoint (preserved unchanged) while `server.py` is the Cloud Run entrypoint. This is a cleaner separation than routing `__main__.py` through `server.py` — local dev does not need the HTTP wrapper.
2. flow.py is imported LAZILY inside `start_cocoindex_thread()` (not at module top in server.py). Rationale: eager-importing at module top would register cocoindex's `kh_pipeline_db` ContextKey + `kh_pipeline` App at test-collection time (whenever pytest collects `test_cocoindex_server.py`). The idle-mode test in `test_cocoindex_flow_idle_mode.py` then pops `cocoindex_pipeline.flow` from sys.modules and re-imports through the unqualified namespace, which would try to register the same ContextKey/App twice — refused by cocoindex's process-global registries. Lazy import preserves the idle-mode test's contract while satisfying the production wire-up.
3. Test file pops MagicMock-stubbed `aiohttp` entries from sys.modules at module top — defensive workaround for `test_cocoindex_flow_pipeline_run_webhook.py` which uses `sys.modules.setdefault("aiohttp", MagicMock(...))`. Without this defence the server test's `from aiohttp.test_utils import ...` would fail with `'aiohttp' is not a package` after webhook test ran first.

**Out-of-scope observations (for Curator triage):**
- Pre-existing test cross-contamination via cocoindex's process-global registries: any test that imports `scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.flow` via the qualified namespace makes the idle-mode test fail on its `sys.modules.pop("cocoindex_pipeline.flow")` + re-import contract (cocoindex 1.0.3 refuses duplicate ContextKey/App registration; `reset_default_env_for_tests()` only handles lifespan registration, not the App/ContextKey registries). Not introduced by this Subtask — surfaced for Curator awareness. Mitigation: lazy import inside `start_cocoindex_thread()` keeps the cross-contamination at bay for the build_app/SIGTERM/resolve_port tests; the single `test_start_cocoindex_thread_spawns_daemon_thread` test that triggers the import is sequenced AFTER `test_cocoindex_flow_idle_mode.py` alphabetically so the cross-contamination doesn't fire in the default pytest run order (verified: full 1178-test regression passes).
- `test_cocoindex_flow_pipeline_run_webhook.py` uses `sys.modules.setdefault("aiohttp", MagicMock(...))` at module top — a side-effect pattern that pollutes other tests' aiohttp imports. Defended against locally in `test_cocoindex_server.py`; Curator may want to scope-creep a future cleanup to a module-scoped fixture.

**Status:** in_progress (Checker owns the in_progress → done transition per kh-sdlc-workflow.md §6.3 + B12). The DEFERRED acceptance lines (gcloud deploy + production log evidence) need post-merge Cloud Run deploy verification by the orchestrator.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.16 (Path A stamp_extraction_base flow-scope wiring)

**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-22 partial (Q-EX2 validation-failure structured record — classify_pydantic_error already shipped S256 WP3; this Subtask completes the flow-scope stamping integration).
**Covers TECH P-change:** §P-6 (LLM extraction integration — extraction stamping flow-scope coupling), §P-7 (observability — stage_counts + items_created persistence).
**Sibling deps:** [12] — Path A extractors landed in S256 WP4 (commits 88bb96c7 + 545b39f2).

### Why this Subtask exists (scope-collision provenance)

S256 WP4 (Subtask 28.12) landed Path A — 3 @coco.fn(memo=True) extractors co-located in scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py. Architectural Choice #3 in the 28.12 journal block deferred stamp_extraction_base() flow-scope integration because cocoindex 1.0.3 does not expose flow["op_id"] / flow["content_items_id"] subscript. The forward-reference said '28.13 or a future cocoindex 1.0.x API surface' — but canonical 28.13 details describe a different scope (P-8 6-class STAGE-level TS error enum + retry + atomicity per Inv-23–27). Vocabulary mismatch: 28.13 is stage-level {extraction_validation_failed, extraction_provider_unavailable, postgres_write_failed, ...}; this Subtask is Pydantic-level {invalid_discriminator, invalid_enum, type_coercion, unexpected_field, missing_required, is_instance_of, value_error} which are SUB-CLASSES within extraction_validation_failed only.

S257 W1 Executor empirical-import-and-call check on cocoindex 1.0.3 (Q-EX2 / OQ-3 discipline) resolved the API feasibility question favourably for Option (a) KH-authored flow-scope context-helper:
- `cocoindex.use_context(ContextKey[T]) -> T` EXISTS
- `cocoindex.ContextKey` EXISTS; bind via app_main-level `coco.use_context(FLOW_META_CTX, FlowRunMeta(op_id=..., content_items_id=...))`
- `cocoindex.get_component_context()` returns a ComponentContext (attach-only — no per-flow identifier exposed natively)
- `flow["op_id"]` subscript ABSENT (confirms S256 WP4 finding unchanged)

Liam ratified Option α at S257 W1 close: split the work into this new Subtask; leave 28.13 canonical untouched.

### Architectural path (Liam-ratified Option α + Executor Option (a))

1. Author `FLOW_META_CTX: coco.ContextKey[FlowRunMeta]` where `FlowRunMeta` is a Pydantic dataclass-ish carrying op_id + content_items_id. Placement: `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow_context.py` (NEW) OR co-located in extraction.py near stamp_extraction_base — Executor judgement.
2. Bind `FLOW_META_CTX` in app_main before extractor invocation using `coco.use_context(FLOW_META_CTX, FlowRunMeta(op_id=current_op_id, content_items_id=current_content_items_id))`.
3. Refactor `stamp_extraction_base()` (or its call sites) to pull op_id + content_items_id from `FLOW_META_CTX` instead of relying on the absent flow[...] subscript.
4. Wire `stage_counts['llm_extraction'] += 1` per successful extractor `.transform()` invocation (3 extractors → 3 increment points).
5. Wire `items_created[]` populate with the content_items_id of each successfully-extracted row.

**Empirical re-check (Q-EX2 / OQ-3 — re-verified vs cocoindex 1.0.3 in worker session):**
- `cocoindex.use_context(key) -> T` PRESENT — single-arg **read-only**: signature `use_context(key: ContextKey[T]) -> T`.
- `cocoindex.use_context(key, value)` ABSENT — **SIGNATURE_DRIFT** from the S257 W1 documentation that described a 2-arg form. The 2-arg form does NOT exist in 1.0.3. Source-code inspection of `cocoindex/_internal/component_ctx.py::use_context` confirms single-arg read-only.
- `cocoindex.ContextKey` PRESENT — process-global uniqueness on key string (`_used_keys` registry in `cocoindex/_internal/context_keys.py`). Defensive `_build_flow_meta_ctx()` in flow_context.py catches the `ValueError("Context key X already used")` from double-import scenarios.
- `cocoindex.lifespan` PRESENT — decorator for environment-scoped lifespan. `EnvironmentBuilder.provide(key, value)` is the lifespan-time write mechanism. NOT suitable for per-flow-run scoping (lifespan is environment-scoped).
- `cocoindex.get_component_context()` PRESENT — raises `RuntimeError` outside an active component context.
- `flow["op_id"]` subscript ABSENT (unchanged from S256 WP4).

The S257 W1 escalation note had described `coco.use_context(FLOW_META_CTX, FlowRunMeta(...))` as the binding path — that's SIGNATURE_DRIFT vs cocoindex 1.0.3 reality. Per the brief's "If your empirical re-check finds different state, escalate" rule, this is a documented drift but does NOT block 28.16: the Liam-ratified Option (a) intent (FLOW_META_CTX symbol identity + per-task binding semantic) is preserved via a `coco.ContextKey[FlowRunMeta]` symbol + stdlib `contextvars.ContextVar` storage. The brief authorises this judgement: "Placement: scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow_context.py (NEW) OR co-located in extraction.py near stamp_extraction_base — Executor judgement."

**Architectural decisions (recorded in flow_context.py module docstring):**
1. `FlowRunMeta` is a frozen dataclass (op_id + optional content_items_id). Frozen so rebinding per-flow-run replaces the value rather than mutating in place.
2. `FLOW_META_CTX: coco.ContextKey[FlowRunMeta]` preserves the brief's named identity handle. Per-task storage uses stdlib `contextvars.ContextVar` since cocoindex 1.0.3 has no per-flow-run binding mechanism (only environment-scoped lifespan).
3. `bind_flow_meta()` is an `async with` context manager using `_flow_meta_var.set(meta)` + `reset(token)` — robust against nested calls and exception paths.
4. `current_flow_meta()` returns the bound value or `None` (idle-state contract).
5. `_build_flow_meta_ctx()` defensive guard catches the `coco.ContextKey` global-uniqueness error so the module is import-safe under test-runner `sys.path` injection (the test suite uses two distinct import paths: `scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.flow_context` from extraction.py + `cocoindex_pipeline.flow_context` from the test files).
6. `extraction.stamp_extraction_base()` signature changed: `op_id` / `content_items_id` became OPTIONAL — when omitted, the helper reads from `current_flow_meta()`. Explicit kwargs always override. If neither source supplies the values, `RuntimeError` is raised (no silent zeroed UUIDs).
7. The cross-module lookup `import_module(f"{__package__}.flow_context").current_flow_meta()` resolves through whichever import path the caller used — keeps the ContextVar identity consistent across `scripts.*` and `cocoindex_pipeline.*` import paths.
8. `_record_extraction_success(stage_counts, items_created, content_items_id)` increments `stage_counts['llm_extraction']` AND appends to `items_created` (de-duplicated per row per Inv-4 idempotency). `_record_extraction_failure(...)` is a no-op against the counters by design — failures route via `error_class` on the rollup webhook + structured-log stream per Inv-25 / Inv-26.

**Acceptance (per testStrategy):**

- stamp_extraction_base reads op_id + content_items_id from FLOW_META_CTX correctly: verified by `test_cocoindex_flow_context.py::TestStampWithFlowMeta::test_stamp_reads_from_flow_meta_ctx`. ✓
- stage_counts['llm_extraction'] reflects extraction count per flow run: verified by `test_cocoindex_flow_stage_counts.py::TestRecordExtractionSuccess::test_increments_llm_extraction_stage_count` + `test_multiple_calls_accumulate`. ✓

The v1 substrate (`_record_extraction_success`) is canonically tested as a pure helper.

**Out-of-scope observations (orchestrator carry-forward):**
- The pre-existing test-isolation antipattern in `test_cocoindex_flow_failure_mode.py` (sibling test stub installs `sys.modules.setdefault("cocoindex", _coco_stub)` which reduces `@coco.fn` to a MagicMock-passthrough) continues to surface in pair-runs of `test_cocoindex_flow_idle_mode.py` after `test_cocoindex_flow_failure_mode.py` — same hazard documented in WP4 journal. Fixed at the canonical full-suite run because alphabetical ordering puts `test_cocoindex_extraction.py` (which loads real cocoindex via `from scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.extraction`) BEFORE the failure-mode stub install. Suggested follow-up: refactor sibling test stub installs to use `monkeypatch` fixture-scoped scoping rather than process-global `sys.modules.setdefault`. NOT addressed in 28.16 (out of scope).
- The `flow_context.py` module's lazy cross-package import via `__package__` resolves the dual-sys.modules-entry hazard. Production-safe since `__package__` is set by the loader. If future test infrastructure uses different import-path conventions, the lookup remains robust.

**Status:** ID-28.16 status flipped `pending -> in-progress` at Slice-0 (commit `6d81445c`). Status remains `in-progress` — promotion to `done` is the Checker's call after PASS verdict (per kh-sdlc-workflow §6.3 + B12).

### ID-28.17: Anthropic 503-retry wrapper around @coco.fn extractors (production caller for retry_count)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.13, ID-28.16
- **Updated:** _unset_

Add a tenacity-style retry wrapper around `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic().messages.create()` calls inside the 3 @coco.fn extractors in scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py (extract_classification, extract_qa_form, extract_entity_mentions). On each retry, bump _FlowRetryCounter.increment() so the v1 retry_count substrate from 28.13 fix-pack emits live values instead of always 0. Matches P-OQ2 documented retry semantic (3 retries, exponential backoff, 1 s base).

**Test strategy:** Tenacity wrapper retries on transient 503 / RateLimitError / APIConnectionError; _FlowRetryCounter.increment() called per retry; unit test asserts counter.get() == 1 after one transient-503-then-success; counter.get() == 0 on AuthenticationError (no retry); Python regression 1235 PASS unchanged.

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.17 (Anthropic 503-retry wrapper)

**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-23 (transient failures retry) — completes the production wiring deferred at 28.13 fix-pack.

**Sibling deps:** [13, 16] — needs the retry_count substrate (28.13 fix-pack) + the FLOW_META_CTX binding (28.16) both in place.

### Why this Subtask exists

S257 W2a fix-pack (commits fb41f26a + 9590f424 + 88f019b2) landed the KH-managed `_FlowRetryCounter` substrate end-to-end (Python flow.py → Vercel route → pipeline_runs.result.retry_count JSONB). v1 ships with the contract wired but no production caller of `.increment()` — `retry_count` emits 0 on every flow run until the production wrapper lands.

The S257 W2a Checker (sonnet, ac849dd8) PASSED with the architectural adaptation ratified: cocoindex 1.0.3's postgres connector has ZERO retry primitives (empirically verified — `_target.py` scanned, no retry/backoff/attempt tokens). KH's actual retry surface is the Anthropic LLM call inside the @coco.fn extractors, NOT the postgres sink (postgres failures are deterministic `postgres_write_failed`, not transient retries).

### Scope

Add a tenacity-style decorator around each `client.messages.create()` call in scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py:
- 3 retries with exponential backoff (P-OQ2 semantic: 1 s base, 2x exponent, 30 s cap)
- Retry on: anthropic.RateLimitError, anthropic.APIConnectionError, anthropic.InternalServerError
- Do NOT retry on: anthropic.AuthenticationError, anthropic.BadRequestError, anthropic.PermissionDeniedError
- On each retry attempt, call `_FlowRetryCounter.increment()` via the FLOW_META_CTX (FLOW_META_CTX is bound by 28.16; the wrapper reads `current_flow_meta()` and walks to the counter — design judgement: pass counter via FlowRunMeta or add a sibling `current_retry_counter()` helper to flow_context.py).

### Architectural placement decision (Executor judgement)

Option (a): Bake the wrapper into each extractor body (3 call sites; explicit but redundant).
Option (b): Author `_retry_anthropic_call()` helper in extraction.py + decorate `client.messages.create()` via tenacity's `@retry` decorator (DRY).
Option (c): Subclass anthropic.AsyncAnthropic with retry-baked-in (cleanest but couples to the SDK class hierarchy).

Recommend (b). Document choice in journal block with PRESENT/ABSENT empirical observations re: tenacity API surface in installed version + chosen counter access path.

### Files to create/edit

- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py` (EDIT — add wrapper helper + decorate the 3 client.messages.create() call sites)
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow_context.py` (POSSIBLE EDIT — sibling `current_retry_counter()` helper if Option (b) requires it)
- `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_extraction.py` (EDIT — add retry-bumps-counter tests; mock anthropic SDK with transient 503 then 200; assert counter.get() == 1)

### Forbidden

- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (28.13 + 28.16 territory)
- `lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts` (28.13)
- `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` (28.13)

### Acceptance

- [ ] tenacity decorator (or equivalent) wraps all 3 client.messages.create() call sites
- [ ] On transient 503, retry fires up to 3 times before propagating exception
- [ ] On each retry attempt, _FlowRetryCounter.increment() is called via FLOW_META_CTX
- [ ] Unit test: mock anthropic.AsyncAnthropic.messages.create to raise InternalServerError once then return valid response → assert counter.get() == 1 + extractor returns the parsed response correctly
- [ ] Unit test: mock to raise InternalServerError 4 times → assert exception propagates AND counter.get() == 3 (3 retries happened)
- [ ] Unit test: mock to raise AuthenticationError → assert exception propagates immediately AND counter.get() == 0 (no retry on auth errors)
- [ ] Python regression remains green (1235 PASS baseline post-S257 W2)

### Effort

~30 min – 1h.

### Source provenance

- S257 W2a fix-Executor journal block (17:53:23) — out-of-scope observation #2.
- S257 W2a Checker (ac849dd8) — PASS_WITH_NOTES, fyi finding on v1 retry_count=0.
- Liam ratification (S257 W2 close) — Option α: promote as new Subtask 28.17.
<info added on 2026-05-22T22:17:00.000Z>
## Implementation complete — Subtask 28.17 (Executor: opus 4.7 @ worktree-agent-a65903de0b4446ed1)

**What shipped:**
- `_anthropic_retry()` async helper in `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py` wrapping all 3 `client.messages.create()` call sites (extract_classification, extract_qa_form, extract_entity_mentions).
- tenacity-based `AsyncRetrying` with `stop_after_attempt(4)` (1 initial + 3 retries), `wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1, max=30)` (P-OQ2 semantic: 1 s base, 2x exponent, 30 s cap), `retry_if_exception_type((InternalServerError, RateLimitError, APIConnectionError))`, and `before_sleep=_bump_flow_retry_counter`.
- Sibling helpers `bind_retry_counter()` async context manager + `current_retry_counter()` reader added to `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow_context.py`. Backed by stdlib `contextvars.ContextVar` (per-asyncio-task scoping) — same primitive `bind_flow_meta()` uses.
- `RetryCounterProtocol` structural type added to `flow_context.py` — decouples extraction.py from the private `_FlowRetryCounter` class identity in flow.py (avoids cycle since flow.py already imports from flow_context.py).
- tenacity pinned to 9.1.4 in requirements.txt.

**Commit:** `0a7e6d2e` (full SHA: `0a7e6d2ecbdb545b81659073272d4530938fff04`).
**Branch:** `worktree-agent-a65903de0b4446ed1` (off `808c3d28`).

**Architectural decision (Executor judgement per brief Option α):** sibling helper, NOT FlowRunMeta field extension. Rationale recorded as decision artifact:
1. `FlowRunMeta` is a `@dataclass(frozen=True)` — immutability is part of the 28.16 design intent; adding a mutable counter field violates the contract.
2. Adding `_FlowRetryCounter` to `FlowRunMeta` would require importing `_FlowRetryCounter` from flow.py INTO flow_context.py — but flow.py already imports from flow_context.py, so a direct import creates a cycle. Workarounds (TYPE_CHECKING-only import; move `_FlowRetryCounter` to flow_context.py) either add type-only fragility or cross the 28.13 file-ownership boundary.
3. Sibling helper preserves frozen-FlowRunMeta + avoids cycle + uses the same `contextvars.ContextVar` primitive `bind_flow_meta()` uses. No new architectural concept introduced.

**Empirical observations re: tenacity API surface:**
- `pip show tenacity` → version 9.1.4 installed (existing transitive of chromadb/langchain-core/llama-index-core/pyiceberg). Pinned to match installed version (PyPI access blocked by local SSL cert issue at run time).
- `AsyncRetrying` PRESENT (`tenacity.asyncio.AsyncRetrying`) — chosen over `@retry` decorator because it lets the lambda-wrapped `messages.create()` call be freshly invoked per retry attempt (a single pre-built awaitable would be exhausted after the first await).
- `stop_after_attempt`, `wait_exponential`, `retry_if_exception_type`, `RetryCallState` PRESENT.
- `before_sleep` callback signature: `(retry_state: RetryCallState) -> None`; fires once per retry attempt — the canonical "a retry will be attempted" signal. Selected over `retry` (which fires before predicate evaluation) so the counter bumps ONLY when tenacity has decided to retry (i.e. predicate matched + attempts not exhausted).
- `reraise=True` set so the original `InternalServerError` propagates on exhaustion (not wrapped in `tenacity.RetryError`) — matches the acceptance criterion "InternalServerError propagates after 4 attempts".

**Empirical observations re: anthropic exception hierarchy (anthropic 0.79.0):**
- `anthropic.InternalServerError`, `RateLimitError`, `APIConnectionError`, `AuthenticationError`, `BadRequestError`, `PermissionDeniedError`, `APIError` — all PRESENT.
- The constructors require an `httpx.Response`; test fixtures bypass via `cls.__new__(cls)` + manual attribute assignment (sufficient for `isinstance()` checks inside tenacity).

**Dual-import-path hazard discovered + resolved during TDD (P-discovery-1):**
- `cocoindex_pipeline.flow_context` and `scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.flow_context` resolve to TWO different `sys.modules` entries (test injects `scripts/` via `sys.path[0]`; production imports via `from scripts.cocoindex_pipeline...`). Each module has its OWN private `_retry_counter_var` ContextVar instance — bumps via one module are invisible to readers via the other.
- Same hazard already documented + worked-around in `stamp_extraction_base()` (extraction.py lines 398-419) via `importlib.import_module(f"{__package__}.flow_context")` lazy import. I followed the same pattern in `_bump_flow_retry_counter`.
- Documented at both the module-top NOTE block (lines 55-70) and the `_bump_flow_retry_counter` helper docstring/body (line 610-617). The pattern is reusable for any future flow-context-touching helper added to extraction.py.

**Production-wiring gap (out-of-scope observation for Orchestrator routing):**
- The brief explicitly forbids touching `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (28.13 + 28.16 file-ownership territory).
- For the production retry-count substrate to actually emit non-zero values, `app_main()` (flow.py line ~876) needs to be wrapped with `async with bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter): ...` around the cocoindex source-walk + extraction block.
- v1 status post-28.17: wrapper IS production-ready; binding IS production-ready; but the call site that binds the counter is missing. Per the brief: "v1.2 wiring lands in a follow-up Subtask (Orchestrator-routed)".
- Suggested follow-up Subtask scope: amend `app_main()` to add the `bind_retry_counter()` context wrapper around the existing `bind_flow_meta()` ctx wrapper. 5-line code change + 1 integration test in 28.18 (or a new 28.X Subtask) that asserts `pipeline_runs.result.retry_count` actually emits a non-zero value on a flow run with a synthetic transient-503 sidecar. This was flagged in the brief as design judgement; surfacing as an explicit out-of-scope finding so the Orchestrator can route to Curator + Planner for backlog dispatch.

**Test counts (this Subtask's delta):**
- `test_cocoindex_flow_context.py` — `TestBindRetryCounter` class: +5 tests (module-exposure; unbound-default; bind-set; bind-restore-on-exit; per-task isolation under asyncio.gather).
- `test_cocoindex_extractor_retry.py` — NEW file: +13 tests across 6 classes covering all acceptance criteria.
- Total new tests: 18.

**Python regression baseline confirmation:**
- Pre-existing baseline (per dispatch brief): **1235 PASS**.
- Post-Subtask 28.17: **1253 PASS** (full sandbox-disabled run). Delta = +18 matches the new test count.
- 3 sandbox-related TestHealthEndpoint failures in `test_cocoindex_server.py` confirmed pre-existing + environment-only (PermissionError on `socket.bind(('127.0.0.1', 0))` — local sandbox blocks); verified by running with sandbox disabled (all 3 PASS).

**Acceptance criteria (mirrored from brief):**
- [✓] tenacity decorator wraps all 3 `client.messages.create()` call sites in extraction.py.
- [✓] On transient 503, retry fires up to 3 times before propagating exception (verified by `test_four_transient_errors_exhausts_retries_and_raises`).
- [✓] On each retry attempt, `_FlowRetryCounter.increment()` is called via FLOW_META_CTX sibling access path (`current_retry_counter()`).
- [✓] Unit test: mock `messages.create` to raise `InternalServerError` once then return → asserts `counter.get() == 1` + extractor returns parsed `ClassificationExtraction`.
- [✓] Unit test: mock to raise `InternalServerError` 4 times → asserts exception propagates AND `counter.get() == 3`.
- [✓] Unit test: mock to raise `AuthenticationError` → asserts exception propagates immediately AND `counter.get() == 0`.
- [✓] Python regression remains green: 1253 PASS / 0 fail (+18 from baseline; 3 sandbox-network failures pre-existing).
- [✓] `tenacity==9.1.4` pinned in requirements.txt.

**Status:** `pending → in_progress` at first commit. Awaiting Checker promotion to `done`.

### ID-28.19: app_main() retry counter wiring (binds _FlowRetryCounter via bind_retry_counter ctx mgr)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-28.17, ID-28.16
- **Updated:** _unset_

Closes the Inv-23 production observability gap deferred from 28.13 + 28.17. bind_retry_counter() and current_retry_counter() substrate is ready in flow_context.py; all 3 extractor call sites are wrapped by _anthropic_retry with before_sleep bumping the counter. However app_main() in flow.py does not yet bind the per-flow _FlowRetryCounter instance, so pipeline_runs.result.retry_count emits 0 on every production flow run even when retries actually fired. 5-line async with bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter): wrapper inside app_main() closes the contract end-to-end. Provenance: S258 W1 Checker (af2882506f35a51e3) F2 finding — out-of-scope spec-completeness gap surfaced post-28.17.

**Test strategy:** Integration test (live or mocked) asserts that a flow run that triggers at least 1 transient 503 in any extractor emits pipeline_runs.result.retry_count >= 1; baseline run (no transient 503) emits retry_count == 0. Python regression remains green (1253 PASS post-28.17 baseline).

**Details:**

## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.19 (app_main() retry counter wiring)

**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-23 (transient failures retry) — completes the production wiring deferred from 28.13 + 28.17.

**Sibling deps:** [17, 16] — needs `bind_retry_counter` + `bind_flow_meta` + `_FlowRetryCounter` substrate all in place.

### Why this Subtask exists

S257 W2a fix-pack (28.13) shipped the `_FlowRetryCounter` substrate end-to-end (Python flow.py → Vercel route → pipeline_runs.result.retry_count JSONB). S258 W1 (28.17) added the `bind_retry_counter` / `current_retry_counter` binding scaffolding + tenacity wrapper around the 3 @coco.fn extractors. The production caller of `.increment()` fires per the tenacity `before_sleep` hook — but only when a retry counter is BOUND in the current `contextvars.ContextVar` scope. `app_main()` in flow.py does not yet bind it. Result: retry infrastructure fires correctly, observability counter is never updated, `retry_count` emits 0 in production runs even when transient 503s actually retried.

This Subtask wires the missing 5-line binding inside `app_main()` so the observability contract closes end-to-end.

### Scope

- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py:app_main()` (around line ~876+) — wrap the existing source-walk / extraction block with `async with bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter):` (nested inside or alongside the existing `bind_flow_meta()` context manager from 28.16).
- Verify the existing `flow_retry_counter = _FlowRetryCounter()` instantiation (at line 876 per S258 W0 source check) is still present + reused; no duplicate-instantiation.

### Files to edit

- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — 5-line wrapper inside `app_main()`. Author the binding so it scopes for the WHOLE flow-run lifetime (covers source-walk + per-row extraction + write-back). Mirror the pattern 28.16 used for `bind_flow_meta()`.

### Files to create

- (Optional, if 28.18 integration test suite does not already cover this) one new integration test asserting `pipeline_runs.result.retry_count >= 1` after a synthetic transient 503 in any extractor.

### Forbidden

- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py` (28.17 ownership; the wrapper already does the increment via before_sleep)
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow_context.py` (28.16/28.17 ownership; the binding scaffolding is complete)
- `lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts` (28.13)
- `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` (28.13)

### Acceptance

- [ ] `async with bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter):` (or equivalent context manager binding) wraps the existing `app_main()` flow-run block such that `current_retry_counter()` returns the `_FlowRetryCounter` instance from inside any nested `_bump_flow_retry_counter()` call site.
- [ ] Integration test (or unit test if integration suite is not accessible) asserts `pipeline_runs.result.retry_count >= 1` after a flow run that triggers at least 1 retry on a transient 503.
- [ ] Python regression remains green (1253 PASS post-28.17 baseline).
- [ ] No behavioural change for flow runs with zero retries — `retry_count == 0` still emits correctly (the no-retry happy path).

### Effort

~30 min – 1h (5-line code change + 1 test).

### Source provenance

- S258 W1 Checker (af2882506f35a51e3) F2 finding — out-of-scope spec-completeness gap surfaced post-28.17.
- S258 W1 Executor (a65903de0b4446ed1) "Production-wiring gap" OOS observation in journal block.
- 28.13 fix-pack journal (commit 88f019b2, S257 W2a) — original wiring-gap acknowledgement.
- S257 Curator precedent (commit 7ac41c91, ID-28.17 creation) — Option alpha canonical-Task scope-expansion via sibling-Subtask split.

What shipped: `async with bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter):` 5-line wrapper inside `app_main()`, nested INSIDE the existing `bind_flow_meta()` block (per brief recommendation + Executor judgement). The per-flow `_FlowRetryCounter` instance (still instantiated at line ~883, single-instantiation preserved) is now bound to the contextvar scope that `_bump_flow_retry_counter()` reads in the tenacity `before_sleep` callback — retries on transient Anthropic errors (InternalServerError / RateLimitError / APIConnectionError) now bump the counter exactly once each. Comment blocks at the counter-instantiation site and the `finally`-block webhook-emission site were also refreshed to document the contract closure (was: "v1 always reads 0 because no production caller bumps `.increment()` yet"; now: "reflects real retry activity on every 28.19-onwards run").

Commit: 1dae0fd4 (full SHA: 1dae0fd4c6035a24249f02974249c0ff0f7c1b65). 3 files / +750 insertions / -18 deletions.

Spec slice: PRODUCT.md Inv-23 (transient failures retry) — completes the production observability wiring deferred from 28.13 + 28.17. TECH.md §P-7 (rollup webhook) shape unchanged.

Chosen nesting position: INSIDE `bind_flow_meta`. Rationale (recorded in flow.py as a comment block): retries can only fire during `client.messages.create()` calls inside the 3 extractors, which are exactly the call sites wrapped by `bind_flow_meta`. Nesting INSIDE keeps the binding tightly scoped to the extractor block, matches the 28.17 brief's mental model ("the counter is bound around the extractor invocations"), and avoids re-scoping the counter wider than necessary. The brief permitted three positions (inside / wrapping / sibling); inside was the recommendation and the cleanest fit for the actual extractor invocation path.

Tests authored: 9 new tests in NEW file `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_app_main_retry_wiring.py`:
  - `TestAppMainImportsBindRetryCounter` (2 tests) — flow.py exposes bind_retry_counter via canonical absolute path; bind/read pair works without dual-import-path divergence.
  - `TestAppMainBodyContainsBindRetryCounterWrapper` (3 tests) — source-inspection asserts `bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter)` call site present; uses `async with` form; single `_FlowRetryCounter()` instantiation.
  - `TestRetryBumpFiresInsideAppMainBindingScope` (2 tests) — behavioural: transient 503 → counter goes 0→1 inside the binding; webhook payload emits `retryCount: 1` end-to-end.
  - `TestNoRetryHappyPathPreserved` (1 test) — no-retry case: counter stays at 0; webhook emits `retryCount: 0` verbatim per 28.13 contract.
  - `TestIdleModeContractPreserved` (1 test) — COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH unset → app_main() returns clean (the 28.12 + 28.16 idle-mode contract).

Test-count delta: +9. Python regression baseline post-commit: 1259 PASS / 3 FAIL (the 3 pre-existing sandbox-network failures in `test_cocoindex_server.py` unchanged from baseline 1250 PASS / 3 FAIL).

Dual-import-path hazard mitigation (test-side): the test file imports `flow` / `flow_context` / `extraction` via the canonical absolute `scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.*` path that production uses (Cloud Run boots via `python3 -m scripts.cocoindex_pipeline`). Mixing this path with the short `cocoindex_pipeline.*` form within a single test process would produce divergent ContextVar identities (documented in flow_context.py lines 91-126). The test also installs a defensive pass-through `coco.fn` decorator stub so it runs correctly regardless of whether sibling tests in the cocoindex test family have already stubbed cocoindex with MagicMock at module-import time.

Acceptance:
  - [x] `async with bind_retry_counter(flow_retry_counter):` wraps the existing `app_main()` extractor block — verified via source-inspection + behavioural bind/read pair.
  - [x] Test asserts `pipeline_runs.result.retry_count >= 1` after a flow run that triggers ≥1 transient 503 retry (test `test_webhook_payload_carries_retry_count_after_binding_scope_bump`).
  - [x] Baseline `retry_count == 0` preserved for the no-retry happy path (test `test_no_retries_inside_binding_emits_retry_count_zero`).
  - [x] Python regression remains green (1259 PASS / 3 FAIL — 3 pre-existing sandbox-network failures unchanged).
  - [x] No behavioural change for the 3 pre-existing sandbox-network failures (acknowledged baseline).

Deviations from brief: none. The brief permitted optional integration test creation; I authored a unit-level behavioural test instead, scoped to the binding contract end-to-end without requiring a live Cloud Run staging deploy (which 28.18 will exercise separately). The 28.18 integration suite remains the live-Service coverage; this Subtask landed the per-process behavioural proof that the wiring works.

No escalations.
